title: Service Rental Car (SRC) / 3rd Party Rental Program / Alternative Transportation / Rideshare
raw_content: Rideshare Hyundai Motor America encourages its dealers to participate and actively utilize Service Rental Cars (SRC), 3rd Party Rentals and Rideshares. These programs are intended to help drive and maintain customer satisfaction by providing service customers with transportation while their vehicle is in service. The expectation for the program is that dealers will present SRC and alternative transportation options to all retail service customers regardless of warranty status or where the vehicle was purchased. The SRC program and alternative transportation options are among the strongest customer retention and satisfaction tools available. Note: The SRC, 3rd Party Rental and Rideshare options are not considered part of the New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW). For further details, including reimbursement rates, best practices and requirements, please refer to the SRC Program Rules available on www.Hyundaidealer.com > Service > Document Library > Service Rental Car. Criteria for Reimbursement For those repairs being performed under the New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW) that meet the specific requirements outlined below, Hyundai Motor America will reimburse the dealer based on a three-tier daily reimbursement rate. For 3rd Party Rentals or Rideshares (i.e., Uber, Lyft) a specific defined daily rate of reimbursement is provided as well. No Prior approval is required for SRCs, 3rd Party Rentals or Rideshares so long as it meets the criteria for reimbursement outlined below. Hyundai Motor America may provide a Service Rental Car (SRC), 3rd Party Rental or Rideshare reimbursement to dealers for customer satisfaction purposes for one or more of the following reasons: • Vehicle is within any warrantable and certain goodwill conditions and the repair cannot be completed in the same day and: – Vehicle is unsafe, inoperative, or requires emergency repairs; or – Non-emergency repairs require the vehicle to be kept overnight to complete. • Parts are on HMA backorder (warrantable concern or customer pay) - Both parts on backorder (B/O) screen printout(s) and part arrival record(s) (for example, WebDCS Parts Order Inquiry screen) be attached to Repair Order, and meets the requirements of: – Vehicle is unsafe, inoperative, or requires emergency repairs; or – Non-emergency repairs require the vehicle to be kept overnight to complete. • Vehicle has multiple warranty issues where total time to complete repairs requires more than one day. • Vehicle has extensive diagnostic time where total time to complete diagnosis and repairs require more than one day. • At the request of HMA’s AMM, FSE, or CA department; alternative transportation can be provided for technical inspection of the subject vehicle. If a dealer cannot duplicate a customer complaint and consequently submits a No Trouble Found (NTF) claim, HMA can reimburse a maximum of two days rental (2 business days, Monday to Friday) . If one or more of the aforementioned bulleted requirements are met, the dealer is eligible to receive reimbursement. The Hyundai SRC will only pay out max of one daily reimbursement rate per day. IE: If a vehicle is used multiple times in one day for warranty related concerns, HMA will only reimburse for one occurrence that day. However, a vehicle used multiple times a day will benefit SRC utilization. Please refer to SRC Program rules for complete details. Dealers are required to complete warranty repairs in a timely manner. Dealers may be denied or charged back reimbursement if it is the result of inadequate dealer scheduling or if the dealer is found not to have accessed available TSB, HTSS, or other HMA technical resources leading to delays in the repair. Backordered parts should be placed on a daily parts order or SOP order. If a dealer is found not to have placed an expedited parts order, the rental is subject to chargeback. Customers are responsible for meeting the requirements of the 3rd party rental agency. Note: Allowance for ordered parts is up to 2 rental days (2 business days, Monday to Friday) after the part arrives for dealer to complete the repair. If allowance is exceeded, dealer must attach supporting documentation to the claim for review. In the event a customer is not offered or initially declines an SRC, 3rd Party Rental, or Rideshare but meets the requirements and later requests one of these services; the SRC, 3rd Party Rental, or Rideshare can be offered and is eligible for reimbursement. Rideshare The dealer is eligible for reimbursement for the use of rideshare services provided on behalf of the customer such as Uber or Lyft up to the published daily rate per day while the customer’s vehicle is in service and meets the criteria for reimbursement outlined in this section. The trips can be to and from the dealership or for any ridesharing trips requested by the customer for the duration of their repairs up to the maximum daily reimbursement amount. Dealer Provided Shuttle Service Dealer provided shuttle service is not eligible for dealer reimbursement. Active SRC units cannot be used as a Shuttle. Reimbursement Criteria Not Met Costs associated with SRC vehicles, 3rd Party Rentals, or Rideshares provided for customers that are outside of any warrantable, certain goodwill, certain customer pay conditions, complimentary maintenance, or for those that do not meet the program criteria for reimbursement are the responsibility of the dealership or customer and will not be reimbursed. Source of Rental Vehicles Hyundai encourages dealers to participate in the SRC program, and when an SRC is not available, then an independent 3rd Party Rental may be provided. Dealers are encouraged to sign up for and utilize the Enterprise ARMS© Dealership platform for 3rd Party Rental services and, if available, request a Hyundai vehicle for the warranty customer. Submission of Service Rental / 3rd Party Rental / Rideshare Claims Documentation & Retention All SRC, 3rd Party Rental and Rideshare claims must be submitted to Hyundai Motor America only if there is a corresponding paid warranty claim, campaign claim or certain customer pay conditions. All program claims are subject to review at time of claim submission. Refer to the Hyundai Warranty Claims Processing Manual for details. For warranty claims or campaign claims, please check the Claim Status Report (answerback) or the Claim Inquiry screen in WebDCS to obtain the causal part number. For SRCs, a copy of the TSD (SRC fleet management tool) Vehicle Use Agreement signed by the customer must be attached to the claim. Please also attach any corresponding documents to justify the rental days submitted. For 3 rd Party Rentals, a copy of the rental agreement or final invoice must be attached to the claim. This must show RO number, customer name, days, rental model, rental VIN, daily rate and total amount. Please also attach any corresponding documents to justify the rental days submitted. A rental justification form may be required for explanation and supporting documents on rentals over 3 days. For Rideshares, a copy of the final invoice/receipt must be attached to the claim. Please also attach any corresponding documents to justify the rental days submitted. Receipts for each ride must be attached to the repair order and maintained with the file copy. Unless otherwise required by applicable state law, claims for SRC, 3 rd Party Rentals, and/or Ride Shares should be submitted at the same time as the corresponding repair claim. Claim submission or resubmission over 30 days is subject to denial. See section 6.0. Claims submitted with incorrect/invalid Repair Claim information (RO number and Causal Part) may go into “hold status” for up to 7 days. If after 7 days the system does not find the corresponding repair claim, the claim will be returned. If dealer notices incorrect information was entered or there is no corresponding claim, such as a buyback situation, please contact the WCC through the Warranty Support Portal or by phone at 877-446-2922 for immediate processing. Note: Chargeback of warranty claim will result in subsequent chargeback of associated SRC / Rental / Rideshare Claim(s). What Is Not Covered Under no circumstances should a claim be submitted to Hyundai for the following reasons: • The vehicle is outside of any warrantable and certain goodwill conditions or does not meet the criteria for claim reimbursement. • The rental vehicle is kept beyond the completion of warranty repairs to the eligible vehicle. When the warranty repairs are completed and the customer is notified, continued use of a rental vehicle is at the expense of the customer or dealership. Any warranty claim submitted to Hyundai should not include charges for continued use. • Over-the-counter and retail parts purchases are not eligible for rental coverage due to parts issues or delay. • Dealer scheduling/time issues or delays in servicing the vehicle are not valid reasons for submitting extended rental coverage. • Extended Service Contracts with rental coverage must be used and documented first before considering HMA goodwill rental. RENTAL REASON CODE RENTAL REASON DESCRIPTION REQUIREMENT FOR USAGE DOCUMENTATION NEEDED R4 Parts on HMA Backorder Parts on Backorder due to Hyundai Motor America backorder status. Additional comments must be entered on claim, including part number and parts order number. Repair issue must affect drivability/safety. Requires copy of parts availability screen attached to R.O. R5 Overnight repair / VOR Part Order / Weekend / Holiday Issue must affect drivability/safety for more than one day of rental coverage, or repair falls over a weekend or Holiday Additional comments must be entered on claim and include which part(s) were SOP. Requires copy of parts order attached to R.O., or detail why customer was provided rental over holiday/ weekend, rather than set up for appointment during normal work week. R6 Extensive Diagnosis / FSE / Fast involved Repair requires extensive diagnosis to determine applicable repair Additional comments must be entered on claim including Techline case #, FSE or FAST involvement. R8 Remedy Not Available Rental was provided due to an open recall without a remedy Additional comments must be entered on claim including remedy not available recall information and related part order information, if applicable. R9 Buyback Vehicle has been in dealership for multiple concerns and rental given as part of customer satisfaction. Additional comments must be entered. If CA case exists, Case # must be included in tech notes with claim. Service Manager must sign R.O. and notate authorizing rental in this case. Note: Allowance for ordered parts is up to two (2) rental days (2 business days, Monday to Friday) after the part arrives for dealer to complete the repair. If allowance is exceeded, dealer must attach supporting documentation to the claim for review.

A dealer cannot duplicate a customer complaint and consequently submits a No Trouble Found (NTF) claim
The customer’s vehicle is in service and meets the criteria for reimbursement outlined in this section (for rideshare services)
coverage_and_reimbursement: HMA will reimburse the dealer based on a three-tier daily reimbursement rate for repairs performed under NVLW that meet specific requirements
A specific defined daily rate of reimbursement is provided for 3rd Party Rentals or Rideshares (i.e., Uber, Lyft)
HMA can reimburse a maximum of two days rental (2 business days, Monday to Friday) for No Trouble Found (NTF) claims
The dealer is eligible for reimbursement for the use of rideshare services provided on behalf of the customer such as Uber or Lyft up to the published daily rate per day
Rideshare trips can be to and from the dealership or for any ridesharing trips requested by the customer for the duration of their repairs up to the maximum daily reimbursement amount
not_covered: The SRC, 3rd Party Rental and Rideshare options are not considered part of the New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW)
Dealer provided shuttle service is not eligible for dealer reimbursement
Active SRC units cannot be used as a Shuttle

Costs associated with SRC vehicles, 3rd Party Rentals, or Rideshares provided for customers that are outside of any warrantable, certain goodwill, certain customer pay conditions, complimentary maintenance, or for those that do not meet the program criteria for reimbursement are the responsibility of the dealership or customer and will not be reimbursed
The rental vehicle is kept beyond the completion of warranty repairs to the eligible vehicle
Any warranty claim submitted to Hyundai should not include charges for continued use of a rental vehicle after repairs are completed and the customer is notified
Over-the-counter and retail parts purchases are not eligible for rental coverage due to parts issues or delay
Dealer scheduling/time issues or delays in servicing the vehicle are not valid reasons for submitting extended rental coverage
Extended Service Contracts with rental coverage must be used and documented first before considering HMA goodwill rental
payment_limitations: HMA can reimburse a maximum of two days rental (2 business days, Monday to Friday) for No Trouble Found (NTF) claims
The Hyundai SRC will only pay out max of one daily reimbursement rate per day
Allowance for ordered parts is up to two (2) rental days (2 business days, Monday to Friday) after the part arrives for dealer to complete the repair

timing_rules: Unless otherwise required by applicable state law, claims for SRC, 3rd Party Rentals, and/or Ride Shares should be submitted at the same time as the corresponding repair claim
Claim submission or resubmission over 30 days is subject to denial
claim_processing_risks: Dealers may be denied or charged back reimbursement if it is the result of inadequate dealer scheduling
Dealers may be denied or charged back reimbursement if the dealer is found not to have accessed available TSB, HTSS, or other HMA technical resources leading to delays in the repair
If a dealer is found not to have placed an expedited parts order, the rental is subject to chargeback
Claim submission or resubmission over 30 days is subject to denial
Claims submitted with incorrect/invalid Repair Claim information (RO number and Causal Part) may go into “hold status” for up to 7 days
If after 7 days the system does not find the corresponding repair claim, the claim will be returned
Chargeback of warranty claim will result in subsequent chargeback of associated SRC / Rental / Rideshare Claim(s)

title: Parts Reimbursement
raw_content: Unless otherwise required by applicable state law, HMA will reimburse the Dealer at the Dealer net price plus an additional parts handling allowance based on the Dealer net price. The Dealer net price is the current purchase price applicable to the Dealer on the repair order (RO) open date. The parts handling allowance is to cover the dealer’s administrative costs associated with the storage and handling of the parts. The parts handling allowance is: • 40% for 2016 - 2026 model year vehicles • 40% for all over-the-counter claims • For new Hyundai dealers as a result of a Buy-Sell: the parts handling allowance shall be the lower of: (1) the parts allowance that the selling dealer received at the time of sale or (2) the median dealer parts allowance in the state in which the dealer operates. For example: Selling dealer is 42% and the median dealer parts allowance in the state is 41%, the buying dealer will receive a 41% dealer parts allowance. • For new Hyundai dealers by appointment of HMA: the parts handling allowance shall be 40% • For all other situations: the parts handling allowance shall be 40%
coverage_and_reimbursement: HMA will reimburse the Dealer at the Dealer net price plus an additional parts handling allowance.
The Dealer net price is the current purchase price applicable to the Dealer on the repair order (RO) open date.
The parts handling allowance is to cover the dealer’s administrative costs associated with the storage and handling of the parts.
payment_limitations: Parts handling allowance is 40% for 2016 - 2026 model year vehicles.
Parts handling allowance is 40% for all over-the-counter claims.

For new Hyundai dealers as a result of a Buy-Sell: the parts handling allowance shall be the lower of (1) the parts allowance that the selling dealer received at the time of sale or (2) the median dealer parts allowance in the state in which the dealer operates.
For new Hyundai dealers by appointment of HMA: the parts handling allowance shall be 40%.
For all other situations: the parts handling allowance shall be 40%.

title: Core Policy and "No Part Number" (NPN) Purchase Reimbursement
raw_content: Specific parts (such as a remanufactured alternator assembly), will have a core charge that is a separate charge from the dealer net part price. Warranty reimbursement will be based upon the current dealer net price for the assembly, not the core value, plus the parts handling allowance applied to the dealer net price. Your parts department can give you information on core reimbursement amounts. Please contact the Dealer Parts Support Call Center at 1-800-545-4515 for status relating to these core exchanges. Your PDC will issue credit for cores on the monthly Dealer Parts Statement. 5.1.2 “No Part Number” (NPN) Purchase Reimbursement The Dealer is to maintain an inventory of new Hyundai Genuine supplied parts in sufficient quantity to enable necessary parts replacements in vehicles serviced by the Dealer. The Dealer is required to use new Hyundai Genuine parts in all warranty and customer satisfaction repairs and in the case of sublet repairs, to furnish the outside sublet repair shop with such new Hyundai Genuine parts as may be required for repairs. There are some supplies which Hyundai does not sell (i.e. battery acid, etc.) and others that Hyundai sells that are also available from other sources, which the Dealer or sublet shop must purchase from a source other than HMA. Reimbursement for such items will be based on the allowances shown in the Warranty Claim Processing Manual, with no additional parts handling allowance. title: 12V Battery Claims (excludes 12V Lithium Batteries)
raw_content: A battery in a stored vehicle is subject to conditions that can reduce its performance and life. Regular monthly battery maintenance must be performed by the dealer to ensure proper operation and optimal battery life for the customer. A vehicle battery that is not adequately maintained during dealer storage can result in reduced battery life and customer dissatisfaction. As a matter of warranty policy (2.3.5), Hyundai does not provide warranty coverage for discharged and/or failed 12V batteries due to lapses in dealer aged inventory maintenance. For more information, please refer to section (2.3.2) Dealer Stock Inspection every 30 days (Monthly) – Not Covered by Warranty. Dealers should inspect batteries within 7 days of arrival from Hyundai. Warranty claims can only be submitted for repairs performed within 7 days of unit delivery at dealers. Claims can only be submitted using approved testing methods. For more information and relevant TSBs, please refer to section (2.3.3) 12V Lead-acid/AGM Battery Inspection and Maintenance. It is the dealer’s responsibility to maintain the battery in good condition while the vehicle is in dealer stock. Maintaining the battery in good condition will minimize the dealer’s battery replacement cost since dealer stock is not covered by Hyundai warranty. The regular upkeep of the battery will also ensure optimal battery performance for the customer. The following TSBs contains battery tester information and warranty related procedures. - 24-EE-014H – 12V BATTERY MAINTENANCE FOR INVENTORY & PDI - 24-EE-013H – WEBDCS AGED INVENTORY – BLUELINK SOC ALERTS - 25-EE-003H – GUIDE TO BATTERY DIAGNOSTIC TSBS Note: The TSBs listed above are subject to change; for the most up-to-date version(s) please visit www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service tab/Service Programs/Hyundai Tech Info/TSB. For unusual battery situations, such as a leaking battery, please contact the WCC using the Warranty Support Portal or by phone at 877-446-2922 for assistance. Dealer Battery Maintenance Schedule (per TSB 24-EE-014H): Note: Hyundai highly recommends storing dealer stock vehicles in “Shipping Mode” to help reduce battery current draw and conserve Battery State of Charge (SOC%) while vehicles are in dealer lot or storage location. For more information on Shipping Mode, please review TSB 24-EE-012H-1. Dealer Option - Covered by Warranty: Within 7 days of vehicle delivery to dealer: Inspect the battery condition using Battery Test/Claim App on GDS Smart, referencing TSB 25-EE-003H. Warranty claim submissions must use the approved process described in the TSB. Take the appropriate action based on the test results: Battery Condition Action D00 or Good Battery (Cadex) No action required (covered under PDI) D01: Parasitic Draw Detected (non-sleep communications modules) D02: Parasitic Draw Detected (Vehicle Device Short) D03: Vehicle in ACC mode depleting battery D04: Vehicle not operated often or Charge & Retest (Cadex) Notes: • D02 tests for inventory units may be submitted to PCR as this is an edge case condition. This code is normally not warrantable for retailed units. • D03/D04 is not warrantable. Diagnose, repair the specified Battery Condition and utilize either of the following methods to charge the battery: - Run at engine idle with the headlights ON or use battery charger; charge time will be dependent upon battery condition and vehicle model Note: Turning the headlights ON induces maximum charging rate of the battery. - Charging can be conducted by automatic regulated battery charger. Hyundai recommends DCA8000, Associated Intellimatic or Schumacher DSR124 chargers. - Check the battery again after charging. Battery test result must yield, “Good Battery”. Warranty claim submission notes: - Save the GDS Battery Diagnosis test result for your records. The results should be uploaded to the server automatically. - For QX Venue, the Cadex tool per TSB 25-EE- 005H, two warranty codes from the battery test result printout below must be submitted with the claim using GDS Smart: o Initial “Charge & Retest” result before charging. This test is sent to servers automatically o “Good Battery” or “Replace Battery” result printout from DCA-8000 after charging must be scanned and submitted to HMA using Battery Test/Claim App. R01/R02/R03/R04 or Replace Battery (Cadex) Replace Battery - Save the GDS Battery Diagnosis test result for your records. The results should be uploaded to the server automatically. - Retain the battery for WTC collections per 24- EE-009H Dealer Required - Not Covered by Warranty: Dealer stock unit battery maintenance for every 30 days (monthly), every 90 days (3 months), and in conjunction with WEBDCS Aged inventory - Bluelink SOC Alerts (TSB 24-EE-013H). – Check the battery condition with Hyundai’s required diagnostic tools: Battery Condition Action Good Battery No action required. Charge & Retest Use either option “A” or “B” below to charge the battery. A. Engine Idle with Headlights ON B. Automatic Regulated Battery Charger Charging time will depend on the battery state of charge (SOC) and vehicle model. After charging, check the battery again with a Hyundai approved battery tester and utilize the Battery SOC information on TSB 24-EE-013H . Battery test results must yield, “Good Battery” Refer to TSB 24-EE-014H and TSB 24-EE-013H for more details. Replace Battery Replace Battery at Dealer’s expense Please note: The TSBs listed above are subject to change; for the most updated version(s), please visit www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service Tab/Service Programs/Hyundai Tech Info/TSB. Note: There will be a warning on the instrument cluster if the vehicle is driven with the Power Fuse Switch turned off.

title: Prior Approval Policies
raw_content: Hyundai’s Prior Approval (PA) Center provides a centralized location that authorizes Warranty and Goodwill Repairs. Prior Approval is required for certain repairs (5.4.1) prior to performing the repair. As a reminder, the required claim preparation procedures, as outlined in other sections of the P&P including other HMA publications (i.e. Campaign Literature, Technical Service Bulletin, etc.) must also be adhered to; a Prior Approval will not serve as a “blanket” approval and the claim may still be subject to denial or chargeback for failure to comply with the claim preparation procedures. Dealer shall adhere to the following: • Submit PA request within 2 business days (Monday to Friday) of R.O. open date. • Respond to PA return within 1 business day (Monday to Friday). • Order part(s) within 1 business day (Monday to Friday) of PA approval. Note: Refer to SRC allowance guidelines for max days allowed for repair completion.

Respond to PA return
Order part(s)

not_covered: A Prior Approval will not serve as a "blanket" approval
Claim may still be subject to denial or chargeback for failure to comply with claim preparation procedures

Order part(s) within 1 business day (Monday to Friday) of PA approval

title: Prior Approval Requirements
raw_content: Dealers are required to submit a Prior Approval for authorization, prior to performing the following repairs: – Engine Assembly (sub engine assembly and short engine assembly) – Transmission/Transaxle Assembly (manual, automatic, IVT and DCT) – Electric High Voltage Battery – Engine Assembly, Transmission Assembly or Electric High Voltage Battery sold to a third-party repair facility – Combustion Chamber Cleaning (Oil Consumption) – All Goodwill related repairs including: o Campaign related repairs (consequential and previously completed recall repairs) o Goodwill over HSA limits (5.4.6) o Concerns reported while vehicle was in warranty o For latest updates, check the WebDCS Messages and Alerts When replacing Engines or Transmissions, dealers must adhere to the Transmission and Engine Programs outlined in sections 5.6.3, 5.6.4 & 5.6.5. HMA reserves the right to furnish the final decisions in all Goodwill matters. HMA encourages Goodwill participation from both Dealerships and/or Customers in the cost of the repairs. HMA may participate only in a portion of the cost of the repairs (i.e. Only Parts or Labor, a Limited Dollar Amount, or a Percentage of the Total Amount). HMA participation in Goodwill repairs will always be Parts at Warranty pricing and Labor times as per HMA LTS, unless otherwise stated or required under applicable state law. Note : If a previously completed Recall needs to be performed again and the vehicle is out of warranty, in order to ensure that the Recall is performed at no expense to the customer, please submit a Prior Approval Request for goodwill consideration prior to performing the work. Check the WebDCS Messages and Alerts for the latest updates.

title: Prior Approval Request Submission
raw_content: Prior to starting any repairs which require Prior Approval (5.4.1), dealers must submit a PA request from WebDCS. To locate the PA Request under WebDCS, find the Service tab, access the PA Request link and then create and submit a new request. Status updates of other PA requests in Approved, Saved, In Progress, Returned or Declined, can also be found under the PA Request link. Comments for the PA Request Reason: • Engine PA Requests Include: – Repairs Needed & Cause of Malfunction – Condition/Level of the Oil/Signs of Sludge/Metal Contamination – New or Reman; include Part Number – Breakdown of Parts and Labor • Transmission PA Requests Include: – Repairs Needed & Cause of Malfunction – DTC Codes – New or Reman; include Part Number – Breakdown of Parts and Labor • Electric High Voltage Battery PA Requests Include: – Techline advised replacement – DTC Codes – Breakdown of Parts and Labor • Goodwill PA Requests Include: – Repairs Needed – Why warranty is not applicable – Reason for the Goodwill Note: The list above is not all-inclusive and is subject to change. Required Attachments: All PA requests require repair order (RO), front & back, including technician comments. Additional required attachments may include photos and documents as requested by PA Center. Submitted photos must be digital, colored, high-resolution, original and unedited. Photocopies of photos are not acceptable. Common photo file formats should be used: e.g. JPEG, PNG, BMP, TIFF. • Engine PA Requests Must Include: – Completed digital Engine Diagnosis Worksheet, including the engine serial number from the removed engine and the Service Manager’s signature. – Copy of customer’s current and up-to-date (not expired) owner registration from Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) to validate 10/100K Powertrain Warranty. Note: Dealers should check that the removed engine core’s serial number matches the OE engine serial number in WebDCS. If it does not match, dealers should notify PA of the removed engine core serial number as part of the PA review/approval process. Failure to notify PA if the engine’s core serial number does not match can result in denial of claim approval or subject to claim chargeback. • Transmission PA Requests Must Include: – Transmission Diagnosis sheet, completely filled out (Use the latest version of the Diagnosis sheet posted in Documents library on www.HyundaiDealer.com) – Copy of customer’s current and up-to-date (not expired) owner registration from Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) to validate 10/100K Powertrain Warranty. ) Note: The list above is not all-inclusive and is subject to change. Please review IMPORTANT NOTES below regarding possible reasons for denial of reimbursement claim: • Failure to confirm vehicle and/or customer eligibility • Failure to submit the required PA Request and obtain Prior Approval can result in denial of the dealership’s reimbursement claim. • Reusing photos from another vehicle will result in denial of the dealer’s reimbursement claim. • Failure to disclose information related to warranty eligibility • Obscene, offensive, lewd or derogatory media submissions (including audio submissions) • Falsified documentation • Using Techline / Field Service Engineering repair recommendation as approval in lieu of approved PA request for warranty coverage or goodwill assistance. Note: The list above is not all-inclusive and is subject to change.

title: Prior Approval For Dealer Buy/Sell
raw_content: In instance of dealer buy/sell, submit a new PA request utilizing the new dealer code for any vehicle where the claim will be submitted after the previous Dealer’s ‘end’ date and a PA number is required on the claim submission (i.e., campaign engine claims). If a new PA is needed, the only required documentation for the new PA submission is a copy of the repair order from the roll-up (new) Dealer. The related supporting documentation will be referenced from the previous Dealer approval. Any pending or new PA requests needed after dealer’s termination date must be submitted as new PA requests utilizing the new dealer code.

title: Hyundai Dealership Self-Authorization (HSA)
raw_content: HMA’s dealer self-authorization program is referred to as HSA (Hyundai Dealer Self- Authorization). This dealer self-authorization semi-annual program enables qualifying and enrolled dealerships to perform specific out-of-warranty and/or selected warranty repairs. In addition, the associated claims for these repairs are processed directly upon claim submission. HSA Dealers may self-authorize Goodwill repairs within the following HSA limits: • Repairs beyond coverage but within 12 months/12,000 miles of New Vehicle Limited Warranty (60 months/60,000 miles) & Powertrain Warranty (120 months/100,000 miles) components* • Total repair amount per visit is $1,000.00 or less *Replacement of engine and transmission assemblies require Prior Approval. If any HSA limits are exceeded, dealers must submit for Prior Approval (5.4.1 & 5.4.2). Goodwill claims exceeding any HSA limits, total repair amount per visit/months/mileage, and submitted without obtaining Prior Approval are subject to denial or chargeback. Examples when HSA Dealers can self-authorize Goodwill repairs: • New Vehicle Limited Warranty Repairs beyond 60 months/60,000 miles, but within 72 months/72,000 miles from date of first use • Powertrain Warranty Repairs beyond 72 months/72,000 miles, but within 72 months/72,000 miles from date of first use, for second and/or subsequent owner * • Powertrain Warranty Repairs beyond 120 months/100,000 miles, but within 132 months/112,000 miles from date of first use, for original owner * • Federal emission repairs beyond 96 months/80,000 miles, but within 108 months/92,000 miles from date of first use • California emission repairs beyond 84 months/70,000 miles, but within 96 months/82,000 miles from date of first use • Parts & Accessories Warranty repairs beyond 12 months/12,000 miles, but within 24 months/24,000 miles from date of original customer purchase • Bluetooth, Navigation, Radio and Sound System repairs: - Beginning with 2016MY: Repairs beyond 60 months/60,000 miles, but within 72 months/72,000 miles from date of first use - Prior to 2016MY: Repairs beyond 36 months/36,000 miles, but within 48 months/48,000 miles from date of first use (excluding 2013-2015MY Equus which is covered for 60 months/60,000 miles) • Maintenance and miscellaneous repairs such as belts/brake pads/bulbs beyond 12 months/12,000 miles, but within 24 months/24,000 miles from date of first use ** Note: The list above is not all-inclusive and is subject to change. *Replacement of engine and transmission assemblies require Prior Approval. **Maintenance repairs for Equus not performed as part of a scheduled maintenance as outlined in the Equus Service Advocate QRG is not warranty, see 4.1.15. Any part being replaced due to wear is not eligible for HSA, see 5.4.8. Note: Eligible HSA self-authorized claims submitted by HSA actively enrolled dealers will automatically be identified and processed as self-authorized claims.
dealer_actions: If any HSA limits are exceeded, dealers must submit for Prior Approval (referencing sections 5.4.1 & 5.4.2).

Total repair amount per visit must be $1,000.00 or less.

title: HSA Eligibility Criteria
raw_content: *HSA Eligibility Criteria are reviewed on a frequent basis throughout the year and are subject to change. Dealership eligibility for enrollment in the semi-annual HSA program is based on the following criteria: 1. Average Warranty Operational Performance Review (WOPR) ranking is in the top 550 of Hyundai Dealers in most recent 6-month period. AND 2. Average Hyundai Service Experience (HVX) greater than or equal to 835 in most recent 6-month period. HSA Dealer eligibility and participation will be reviewed and determined semi-annually by HMA. A Dealer that has been previously removed from participation in HSA is eligible for reinstatement (participation) in HSA upon the Dealership meeting or exceeding the HMA Service Objectives, indicated above, for the period under review. Region Management can deny the participation in HSA of any “eligible” Dealership, at any time, provided that Region Management has “just cause” for such denial, such as: • Excessive number of vehicles bought back involving Dealership • New Service Management at Dealership HSA participation start date(s) and removal date(s) are based upon date of repair. Claims meeting HSA parameters with repair completion dates prior to the effective date of active participation will not be processed/accepted as HSA claims. Claims meeting HSA parameters with repair completion dates after the effective date of removal from HSA participation will not be processed/accepted as HSA claims. Claims submitted by participating HSA Dealers that exceed the HSA program self-authorization parameters, will automatically be reviewed by the PA Center for authorization/approval or denial.

title: HSA Decision Guidelines
raw_content: The following types of repairs are to be considered under HSA (Dealer Self-authorization): • Goodwill repairs to be considered only on a case-by-case basis • Warranty repairs must be the direct result of a defect in Hyundai supplied material or Hyundai Factory workmanship • Replacement of a complete assembly with a new assembly when a remanufactured assembly is either not available or the applicable State disclosure laws for a “new” vehicle requires replacement using a new, rather than a used part • Goodwill cost splits/sharing (Customer and/or Dealer contributions to repair cost) • Warranty extension repairs (e.g. clock spring, MDPS Coupling) The following types of repairs/services are NOT to be considered under HSA (Dealer Self- Authorization): • Reconditioning of a used vehicle or repairs to a vehicle in Dealer stock • Repairs for excess expense due to Dealership Technician or Sublet Shop error or inefficiency • Goodwill repairs for a relative, or friend, when such a relationship is the sole basis for the goodwill repair • Repairs to a vehicle for which the actual mileage cannot be determined • Repairs to a vehicle that has a canceled warranty • Repairs to a vehicle that has been abused or used in a manner that is inconsistent with “normal” driving conditions or use • Repairs or components which are not covered under HMA warranty (e.g. Tires) • Repairs that clearly are outside of the warranty period that are due to vehicle: – Abuse – Commercial use – Modification from original factory specification – Normal wear or maintenance related (e.g. fluid changes, belts, spark plugs, brake pads and oil changes-including additional services not covered under the Equus Maintenance Program and Genesis Maintenance Program) – Not being maintained – Covered under an existing Extended Service Contract or similar plan – Deductible for repairs (e.g. repairs covered by Extended Service Contract or Insurance plans) – Gifts, Gift cards or Coupons Note: The list above is not all-inclusive and is subject to change.

title: Anti-Perforation
raw_content: If a vehicle has a rust hole (from the inside out), through a body panel from corrosion due to defects in material or workmanship, the following procedures must be followed: • The Dealer is to inspect the vehicle to determine if the rust is due to a perforation condition. If there is a perforation, the Dealer must complete the Anti-Perforation Limited Warranty Vehicle Inspection Report (NP430-00000_10-04), available on www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service tab / Dealer Resources / Documents Library /Warranty / Anti-Perf Inspection Report (NP430-00000_10-04). The Anti-Perforation Limited Warranty Vehicle Inspection Report should be attached to the corresponding repair order. If the Inspection Report is not available upon request, the claim may be subject to chargeback. Note: Surface corrosion or surface rust is covered for the first 12 months from the date of original retail delivery or date of first use or 12,000 miles, whichever occurs first. • The Dealer should submit the anti-perforation claim to HMA following normal WebDCS claim submission procedures. – Causal Part The causal part is the part number of the panel that caused the owner’s complaint and the need for the repair. If more than one panel is affected, the panel that has the majority of the perforation evident should be listed as the causal part. – Nature and Cause Code Use the applicable nature and cause codes, such as A12 and ZZ8. – Sublet Code Use B2 - body repair. • The Anti-Perforation Limited Warranty Vehicle Inspection Report, repair estimate and repair final invoice should be attached to the hard copy of the repair order and retained by the Dealer following normal warranty document retention policy and procedures.
dealer_actions: Inspect the vehicle to determine if the rust is due to a perforation condition
Complete the Anti-Perforation Limited Warranty Vehicle Inspection Report
Submit the anti-perforation claim to HMA following normal WebDCS claim submission procedures

Retain the Anti-Perforation Limited Warranty Vehicle Inspection Report, repair estimate, repair final invoice, and hard copy of the repair order following normal warranty document retention policy and procedures

title: Dealer Direct Exchange (DDE) Audio Video Navigation Systems
raw_content: 1. The dealer direct exchange manufacturer will stock and ship all remanufactured audio video and navigation units. Ordering of these units will take place using the DCS system. Audio Video Navigation Resources are available at Hyundaidealer.com>Parts>Document Library > Reference Materials > Audio Video Navigation Return and Replacement Program To Exchange Unit (in warranty and out of warranty units) – At time of shipment, HMA will invoice the Dealer at zero ($0.00) cost for the DDE unit. If the order is for an out of warranty unit, a Repair Fee will be charged at time of shipment. • If the inoperative (core) unit is returned to the manufacturer within 45 calendar days of DDE unit ship date, no subsequent charges will be filed. • If the core is not returned to the manufacturer by the 45th calendar day of DDE unit ship date, the DDE unit’s Dealer Net Price will be charged on the 46th day. • If the core is returned to the manufacturer between 46 – 90 calendar days of DDE unit ship date, the DDE unit’s Dealer Net Price will be credited, and a Late Return Fee will be charged. • If the core is returned to the manufacturer after 90 calendar days of DDE unit ship date, the core will not be accepted, and a credit will not be issued. – When returning the core, include a copy of the dealer Repair Order showing the VIN, mileage, and installed part number. 2. Package the inoperative core unit in the carton in which the remanufactured replacement unit was shipped. Then immediately ship, using the provided prepaid FedEx Ground shipping label, the inoperative core, along with a copy of the dealer Repair Order to the manufacturer. The unit will be processed upon receipt and confirmation that the repair falls within the warranty guidelines of the Dealer Direct Exchange AVN Program. – A Repair Fee will be charged for units found to be unwarrantable (out of warranty, modified, damaged due to customer abuse or misuse). 3. Do not attempt any type of repair on these units. If a CD/DVD or any other object becomes stuck or trapped in the unit, do not attempt to remove it . If the customer requests that their CD or other object be returned, fill out the diagnostic form and check the appropriate box in the customer complaint analysis section, requesting the CD/DVD or other object be returned to your dealership, so you may return the item(s) to the customer ( Damage from forcible removal will negate the warranty ). Otherwise, the CD or other object will be disposed of. If the dealership believes that the damage is the customer’s responsibility, the dealership should discuss the matter with the customer. In many cases of customer caused damages, the customer’s auto insurance policy may provide the customer with coverage. HMA recommends that the Dealer communicate this to the customer. Customer caused damage is not warrantable. 4. Submit a warranty claim through the DCS system for the replacement labor. A. Use the labor operation code from HMA LTS that is applicable to the VIN and DDE unit part number, description: radio assembly, qty: 1. Do not use any other labor operation in addition, or substitution, to the one prescribed above. B. Do not charge out the DDE unit as a detail part. If you return the core with 45 calendar days from ship date, you will not be charged for the part. If the core is not returned by the 45th day from ship date, you will be charged for the Dealer Net cost of the replacement radio on your parts statement separately. Audio video and navigation core units are to be returned via prepaid shipping label (FedEx Ground) provided by the manufacturer. For all audio video and navigation replacements, the following must be adhered to: – DDE units must be used unless any of the following are true: • Vehicle has less than 500 miles. • Specific Legal requirement by State Governing Agency. • DDE unit is unavailable or on backorder (parts availability screen print must be attached to the subject repair order to document this condition). • Repeat repair or customer satisfaction - Regional approval is required. – Please note: If dealer substitutes a float unit for an OE unit, DDE billing rules and core return procedures will apply. 5. DDE Repair Facilities • LG/Specmo - Phone: 800-545-7910 or customerservice@specmo.com • MPA/MRC - Phone: 909-456-3798 or RC1_MRC@mobisusa.com • Harman/United Radio - Phone: 800-448-0944 or contact@unitedradio.com

title: Transmission Reman Program
raw_content: For all Transmission replacements, the following must be adhered to: • Remanufactured transmission must be used unless any of the following are true: – Vehicle has less than 500 miles. – Specific Legal requirement by a state governing agency. – Reman part is unavailable or on backorder (parts availability screen print from WebDCS must be attached to the subject repair order to document this condition). - Reman part ordered but system converted to new part order due to no reman inventory available at the facing PDC (the order status inquiry screen in WebDCS that shows that the OE part has supersession with an Advice Code “A” must be attached to the subject repair order to document this condition.) The process to submitting a screenshot is as follows: 1. Go to HyundaiDealer.com/WebDCS/Orders/Order Status Inquiry 2. Select the Original Order Number submitted 3. Take a screenshot and attach it to the RO By utilizing Hyundai’s DCS Online Website at www.HyundaiDealer.com , click on WebDCS, Parts tab, Core Returns to enter core returns and request pickups. MOBIS Parts America’s Dedicated Delivery Service carriers (MPA DDS) will arrange for pick-up and delivery to a MPA DDS core return center where the cores will be inspected, processed, credits authorized, and electronically transmitted to HMA. Do not submit a Warranty Freight Claim for these charges. As HMA is paying all Freight Charges under this program, it is important to note the following Program Guideline Updates: • Go to www.HyundaiDealer.com , then click on WebDCS, Parts tab, Core Returns to initiate the core pickups through MPA DDS. • The Reman Vendor will not accept any direct shipments of transmissions from dealers. Use the MPA DDS program for all transmissions, including WTC returns. • If you have any problems using the website, call your Hyundai Parts Dealer Support Group at 1-800-545-4515 or MOBIS Parts Portal • Dealers should remember to print and include with their pickups, the Bill of Lading (BOL) and Packing List from the Website. This will assist in timely processing. • A returned transmission core should be fully assembled, including the torque converter, motors, actuator, etc. and all plastic/rubber plugs, and the core should be completely drained of all fluids. • Include the following completed forms with the returned core. Failure to adhere to these requirements will result in the chargeback of the core credit and possibly the warranty claim. – Transmission Diagnosis Worksheet. Worksheets are available on Hyundaidealer.com/WebDCS/Parts/Core Returns Diagnosis Worksheets. It is also available on www. HyundaiDealer.com/Service/Documents Library/Service/Reman ATM and Engine Core Return and Diagnosis Forms – Core Return Form – Warranty Parts Tag (part number NP445-00011). Sample in section 7.3. – Dealer’s Repair Order must include detailed condition, cause and correction information. – Final Invoice (if over the counter sale). • Additionally, if a warranty claim is submitted and the transmission core was not returned to a MPA DDS core return center, the warranty claim will be charged back to the dealer. • Dealers will be billed the freight pickup & recovery costs for all invalid returned cores. Before and after readings/measurements must be recorded on the Transmission Diagnosis Worksheet whenever the GDS is used. Second and subsequent remanufactured transmission replacements on the same VIN will require a case to be opened with Tech Line 1-800-325-6604 prior to repair and Prior Approval Center approval/authorization for the claim payment. Your adherence to program guidelines will ensure the program continues to run effectively. If you have any questions concerning the Reman Core Return Program, contact your HMA Parts Dealer Support Center at 1-800-545-4515.

Take a screenshot (of the Order Status Inquiry screen).
Utilize WebDCS, Parts tab, Core Returns to enter core returns and request pickups.
Initiate core pickups through MPA DDS via WebDCS, Parts tab, Core Returns.
Use the MPA DDS program for all transmissions, including WTC returns.
Print the Bill of Lading (BOL) and Packing List from the Website and include with pickups.
Ensure a returned transmission core is fully assembled, including the torque converter, motors, actuator, etc. and all plastic/rubber plugs, and completely drained of all fluids.
Include completed forms with the returned core.
Record before and after readings/measurements on the Transmission Diagnosis Worksheet whenever the GDS is used.
Open a case with Tech Line 1-800-325-6604 prior to repair for second and subsequent remanufactured transmission replacements on the same VIN.
Obtain Prior Approval Center approval/authorization for the claim payment for second and subsequent remanufactured transmission replacements on the same VIN.
Call your Hyundai Parts Dealer Support Group at 1-800-545-4515 if you have problems using the website.
Contact your HMA Parts Dealer Support Center at 1-800-545-4515 if you have questions concerning the Reman Core Return Program.

Remanufactured transmission must be used unless the Reman part is unavailable or on backorder.

title: Engine Reman Program
raw_content: For all engine replacements, the following must be adhered to: • Remanufactured engine must be used unless any of the following are true: – Vehicle is covered under the 5 year 60,000 miles New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW)-use new engine, see section 5.6.5. – Specific Legal requirement by a state governing agency. – Reman part is unavailable or on backorder (parts availability screen print from WebDCS must be attached to the subject repair order to document this condition). - Reman part ordered but system converted to new part order due to no reman inventory available at the facing PDC (order status inquiry screen in WebDCS to show that the OE part has supersession with an Advice Code “A” must be attached to the subject repair order to document this condition.) 1. Go to HyundaiDealer.com/WebDCS/Orders/Order Status Inquiry 2. Select the Original Order Number submitted 3. Take a screenshot and attach it to the RO By utilizing Hyundai’s DCS Online Website at www.HyundaiDealer.com, click on WebDCS, Parts tab, Core Returns to enter core returns and request pickups. MPA DDS will arrange for pick-up and delivery to a MPA DDS core return center where the cores will be inspected, processed, credits authorized, and electronically transmitted to HMA. As HMA is paying all Freight Charges under this program, it is important to note the following Program Guideline Updates: • Go to www.HyundaiDealer.com , then click on WebDCS, Parts tab, Core Returns to initiate the core pickups through MPA DDS. • The reman vendor will not accept any direct shipments of Engines from dealers. Use the MPA DDS program for all engines, including WTC returns. • If you have any problems using the website, call your Hyundai Parts Dealer Support Group at 1-800-545-4515 or MOBIS Parts Portal • Dealers should remember to print and include with their pickups, the Bill of Lading (BOL) and Packing List from the Website. This will assist in timely processing. • Damage to the return core due to improper removal process or insufficient packaging may lead to chargeback of the core charge (if applicable) and warranty claim. Refer to section 7.5.1 for details on Engine Shipping Procedure. • If a warranty claim is submitted and the Engine Core was not returned to a MPA DDS core return center, the warranty claim will be charged back to the dealer. • Dealers will be billed the freight pickup & recovery costs for all invalid returned cores. Second and subsequent remanufactured engine replacements on the same VIN will require a case to be opened with Tech Line 1-800-325-6604 prior to repair and Prior Approval Center approval/authorization for the claim payment. Your adherence to program guidelines will ensure the program continues to run effectively. If you have any questions concerning the Reman Core Return Program, contact your HMA Parts Dealer Support Center at 1-800-545-4515.

title: New Engine/Transmission Replacement
raw_content: Replacement of an engine while the vehicle is covered under the 5 year 60,000 miles New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW) will be done with a new engine. If a new engine is not available, a reman engine may be used with customer’s written consent. Second and subsequent engine and transmission replacements on the same VIN will require a case to be opened with Tech Line 1-800-325-6604 prior to repair and Prior Approval Center approval/authorization for the claim payment. Replacement of an engine for a retailed vehicle outside of the 5 year 60,000 miles NVLW will be done with a reman engine except in the case of any specific legal requirement by a state government agency. Refer to section 5.6.4 for the complete engine reman program policy. Replacement of a transmission for a retailed vehicle will be done with a reman transmission except in the case of any specific legal requirement by a state government agency. Refer to section 5.6.3 for the complete transmission reman program policy.
dealer_actions: For second and subsequent engine and transmission replacements on the same VIN, open a case with Tech Line prior to repair.
For second and subsequent engine and transmission replacements on the same VIN, obtain Prior Approval Center approval/authorization for the claim payment.
Obtain customer’s written consent if a new engine is not available and a remanufactured engine is to be used for a vehicle covered under NVLW.
eligibility_conditions: Vehicle is covered under the 5 year 60,000 miles New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW).
Second and subsequent engine and transmission replacements on the same VIN.
Engine replacement for a retailed vehicle outside of the 5 year 60,000 miles NVLW.
Transmission replacement for a retailed vehicle.
coverage_and_reimbursement: Replacement of an engine with a new engine (if covered under NVLW).
Replacement of an engine with a remanufactured engine (if a new engine is not available and customer provides written consent, or if outside of NVLW).
Replacement of a transmission with a remanufactured transmission.
Engine or transmission replacement with a new unit if specifically required by a state government agency.
payment_limitations: New engine replacement is limited to vehicles covered under the 5 year 60,000 miles New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW).

Engine replacement for retailed vehicles outside of the 5 year 60,000 miles NVLW will be done with a remanufactured engine, except in cases of specific legal requirements by a state government agency.
Transmission replacement for retailed vehicles will be done with a remanufactured transmission, except in cases of specific legal requirements by a state government agency.
documents_required: Customer’s written consent (if a remanufactured engine is used due to new engine unavailability for a vehicle covered under NVLW).
comment_requirements: Reference the Tech Line case number for second and subsequent engine and transmission replacements on the same VIN.

title: Items Considered Dealer Responsibility, Not Warranty
raw_content: The following repair items are considered to be Dealer responsibility and are not covered under warranty: • Inspections included and covered under the Pre-Delivery Inspection Service. • Minor paint and cosmetic concerns which were not noted at time of delivery of the vehicle to the dealership. • Disposal fees incurred. • Defects due to long storage or poor storage conditions caused by lapses in aged inventory maintenance. • Repairs or replacements made necessary by faulty diagnosis or poor workmanship by dealer personnel, or duplicate, repeat, or comeback/subsequent repairs of the same item if previously performed by the same Dealer, unless the replaced part proves to be defective in material or workmanship. • Repairs related to the replacement of parts which are not held for inspection by HMA, or are not returned to HMA when requested, or otherwise not handled in accordance with current HMA policies and procedures. Failure of the following components and assemblies are considered the Dealer’s responsibility and are not reimbursable under warranty if the vehicle involved has been in Dealer inventory for an extended period of time (over 90 days): – Batteries – Paint repairs – Bright metal repairs (chrome, etc.) – Wiper blades – Weatherstrips – Soft trim – Lamp bulbs – Failure of powertrain components due to corrosion – Faded or corroded mirrors, lamps, reflectors and lenses – Fuel system corrosion or defects caused by contamination
dealer_actions: Hold parts for inspection by HMA.
Return parts to HMA when requested.
Handle parts in accordance with current HMA policies and procedures.
eligibility_conditions: Vehicle involved has been in Dealer inventory for an extended period of time (over 90 days).
not_covered: Inspections included and covered under the Pre-Delivery Inspection Service.
Minor paint and cosmetic concerns which were not noted at time of delivery of the vehicle to the dealership.
Disposal fees incurred.
Defects due to long storage or poor storage conditions caused by lapses in aged inventory maintenance.

Duplicate, repeat, or comeback/subsequent repairs of the same item if previously performed by the same Dealer, unless the replaced part proves to be defective in material or workmanship.
Repairs related to the replacement of parts which are not held for inspection by HMA.
Repairs related to the replacement of parts which are not returned to HMA when requested.
Repairs related to the replacement of parts which are otherwise not handled in accordance with current HMA policies and procedures.
Failure of Batteries if the vehicle has been in Dealer inventory for over 90 days.
Paint repairs if the vehicle has been in Dealer inventory for over 90 days.

title: Items Considered Customer Responsibility
raw_content: The following items are considered to be the customer’s responsibility and are not covered under warranty, unless the items are defective in material or workmanship: • Normal maintenance service: oil & filter changes, engine tune-ups, anti-freeze/coolant, refrigerant, head light aiming, tire rotation, wheel alignment, fuel system cleaning beyond 500 miles of service on a new vehicle, wheel balancing, and any maintenance or inspection service recommended by HMA for the vehicle involved. • Defects judged by HMA to be attributable to failure to properly perform the periodic inspections and services as specified in the Owner’s Manual. • Normal Replacement of Service Items – Replacements suggested by Dealer personnel as a part of preventive maintenance – Replacements required because of abuse, misuse, or improper driving habits of the customer – Worn clutch linings, brake linings, and wiper blades – Filter replacements (fuel, oil, and air) – Spark plugs – Condensers – Drive belts • Upholstery and Trim – Worn – Soiled – Torn or cut by a foreign object – Normal deterioration due to use and exposure • Paint – Dents, nicks, scratches or other damage – Normal deterioration due to exposure – Industrial fallout which occurs after delivery of the vehicle to the customer – Chipping due to road conditions or hazards (e.g. sand) • Bright Metal Trim – Dents, nicks, scratches, or other damage – Normal deterioration due to exposure • Damage or Failure Resulting from – Negligence or improper maintenance as specified in the Owner’s Manual – Misuse, accident, theft or fire – Use of improper or insufficient fuel, fluids, or lubricants – Modification, alteration, tampering, or improper repair – Racing or a competitive event (competitive events are defined as formal or informal time trials, competition with another vehicle, or any abnormal application of stress to the vehicle or the components thereof) – Any device and/or accessory not approved and/or supplied by Hyundai and not installed by Hyundai or an authorized Hyundai Motor America dealer – Slight irregularities not recognized as affecting the quality or function of the vehicle, such as slight noise or vibration, or items considered characteristic of the vehicle • Adjustments Required through Normal Use – Pre-Delivery type adjustments required after a reasonable period of normal use – Adjustment or repair to improve the vehicle’s appearance or performance beyond what is considered normal standards – Adjustment performed for a second time by the same Dealer

title: Other Items Not Considered HMA Responsibility
raw_content: The following additional repair items are NOT considered to be HMA responsibility and are NOT covered under warranty: • Tires are warranted by the tire manufacturer and the warranty is administered by the tire distributor and their retail store representatives. • Any HMA product which has been damaged from accident, fire, or other casualty, or which fails due to misuse, negligence, alteration, or overloading beyond the specified vehicle weight rating, or which has been used for purposes other than those generally accepted applications for which it was designed. • Any labor operation performed by other than an authorized Hyundai Motor America dealer, except repairs sublet by the authorized Hyundai Motor America Dealer to a specialty shop or performed in an emergency by an independent repair facility. Any claim to HMA for sublet work must be filed by the Dealer who sublets the work, with the sublet final invoice attached to the dealer accounting copy of the repair order and a copy of the sublet final invoice attached to the shop hard copy of the repair order retained in the customer service file. • Parts not acknowledged by HMA to be defective in original workmanship and/or material. • Glass breakage or scratches, unless conclusive evidence is presented to substantiate the claim that breakage was unquestionably due to a defect in material and/or workmanship. • Repairs required as a result of using any component or parts not recommended or not approved by HMA. • Defects in equipment, devices, and accessories, other than those supplied by HMA and mounted on vehicles by authorized Hyundai Motor America dealers or HMA, and defects resulting from the use of such unspecified devices and accessories. • Concerns and/or difficulties which are generally not accepted as caused by a defect in material or workmanship, which happen in very special operations such as: – Slight sound or vibration which is not recognized as affecting the quality or function of the vehicle – Slight misting of oil or the like around packing and/or oil seals which causes no appreciable consumption or dripping of oil – Gaps between panels which are within the limits set by HMA – External appearance defects such as paint, chrome, and trim, which are not visible unless magnified by special means • Loss of use of the vehicle, loss of time, loss or damage to personal property or revenues, inconvenience and other consequential damages such as expenses for fuel, telephone, travel, lodging, and towing, (except where such exclusions or limitations are not permitted by law). – Certain expenses may be covered by the Hyundai Roadside Assistance Program. Customers should refer to their Hyundai Roadside Assistance Program Owner’s Guide or the Hyundai Owner’s Handbook for these coverages • Key fob reprogramming should only be necessary when a new key is required, thus any labor-only key fob claim will be denied. • Shop materials which are considered Dealer responsibility such as rags, shop cleaner, etc. • Disposal fees incurred are considered the cost of doing business and are therefore considered Dealer responsibility. • HMA reserves the right to return any claim for a VIN with an open campaign.

title: Labor Reimbursements
raw_content: HMA will reimburse the Dealer for labor based upon the Dealer’s approved warranty labor rate in effect on the date of repair, multiplied by the time allowed for the operation, as indicated on your claim and shown in the current Labor Time Standard in effect on the date of the repair. If the labor operation performed is not listed in the Labor Time Standard or in a separate HMA service communication (i.e. Technical Service Bulletin), or if the labor operation is an Actual Time (TT) operation, the Dealer will be reimbursed on the basis of: • Actual labor operation performance time within reasonable time limits, multiplied by the Dealer’s approved warranty labor rate. Service Manager approval must be obtained prior to performing all actual time repairs and must sign his or her name, print his or her name and note the time authorized prior to performing all actual time diagnosis or repairs. In addition, the Technician must provide an explanation of why the actual time is needed on the hard copy or the repair order. The time must be realistic and consistent with accepted industry practices. Actual time added to a standard labor operation to supplement the standard time may only be claimed for highly unusual repair situations where additional time is required to properly complete the repair procedure. The same documentation and authorization requirements apply. ACTUAL TIME REPAIR OPERATION CODE: (NTT VS ATT) Actual Time Repair Operation means any specific repair performed where no applicable standard labor code exists in the manual or additional time is needed for a specific repair: The operation code for Actual Time must be created as follows: 1. “NTT” – NO APPLICABLE STANDARD LABOR CODE EXISTS IN LTS OR LABOR OPERATION IS NOT APPLICABLE TO MODEL Applicable “NTT” labor operation must always be in the first position when submitting a claim. The first five (5) digits of the causal part number or replaced part is used for creating your applicable “NTT” Labor Operation. Example of Causal Part Number: 91837-XXXXX To create the applicable labor operation, use the first 5 digits of the causal part number or replaced part number followed by “NTT”. Example of Causal Part Number: 91837-XXXXX Labor Operation: 91837-NTT 2. “ATT” – ADDITIONAL TIME REQUIRED TO PERFORM REPAIR Applicable “ATT” labor operation must always be in the second or subsequent position when submitting a claim. The first five (5) digits of the specific labor operation requiring additional time is used for creating your applicable “ATT” Labor Operation. Example of labor operation: 56910R00 To create the applicable labor operation, use the first 5 digits of the specific labor operation followed by “ATT”. Example of Causal Part Number: 56900-29750 Labor Operation: 56910R00 ATT Labor Operation: 56910ATT 3. ALL CLAIMS WILL REQUIRE COMPLAINT, CAUSE, AND CORRECTION (“3 C’S”) OR WILL BE RETURNED. Note: Labor operations performed that are not listed in the Labor Time Standard are considered actual time labor operations (TT time). Technicians must record their actual beginning and ending times for all actual time repairs by punching on and off of a Daily Time Ticket (flag sheet). A separate flag is required to document the actual time spent by the technician to make the needed repairs. The time flag strip must be adhered to the back of the repair order hard copy. For dealers with an ERO system, the technician must open a new line and log on and off to document the actual time spent repairing the vehicle.

Service Manager must sign his or her name, print his or her name and note the time authorized prior to performing all actual time diagnosis or repairs
Technician must provide an explanation of why the actual time is needed on the hard copy or the repair order
Actual Time Repair Operation code must be created as "NTT" or "ATT"
If "NTT", the first five (5) digits of the causal part number or replaced part is used for creating the labor operation
Applicable "NTT" labor operation must always be in the first position when submitting a claim
If "ATT", the first five (5) digits of the specific labor operation requiring additional time is used for creating the labor operation

title: No Trouble Found Procedure
raw_content: HMA will reimburse the Dealer for specific diagnosis time for “No Trouble Found”, when no repair (claim has no parts, sublet, towing, freight, or any additional repair labor operation) is performed. Repairs that qualify for diagnosis labor time reimbursement, when no problem/trouble is found and no repair is performed, must adhere to the following requirements: • Repair attempts eligible for no trouble found diagnosis must be related to the following repair categories: – Automatic Transmission – Engine Electrical – Electrical • The customer’s complaints must be fully documented/explained on the repair order. • Normal Hyundai documentation/paperwork requirements apply, with the hard copy of the repair order retained for future review. • Diagnosis performed must directly relate to the Customer’s complaint. • The Technician must provide an explanation of diagnostic procedures and results on the hard copy of the repair order. • Approved diagnosis labor time applies to use of only the following diagnosis equipment: – Global Diagnostic System (GDS) – Hyundai Automatic Transaxle Tester • Reimbursement time is equal to the applicable labor time listed in current Labor Time Standard (LTS) for the labor time operation codes for the Diagnosis operations performed. • Before and after readings/measurements obtained from the above diagnosis equipment must be documented and appear on the Technician’s shop copy of the repair order. All GDS diagnosis usage must have a printout of the before and after readings attached to the Technician’s shop copy of the repair order. • HMA considers fuse failure to be caused not by the fuse itself, but because of an inherently larger issue. Technical support from Techline may assist in finding an actual failure or defective part. Fuses must be claimed under No Trouble Found using NTF0000000 as the causal part. Excessive and/or above average requests for “No Trouble Found” diagnosis time will be continually reviewed by HMA.

title: Repairs on Used Hyundai Vehicles in Dealer Inventory
raw_content: The phrase, “Repairs on used Hyundai Vehicles in Dealer Inventory” refers to repairs performed on any used Hyundai taken into a Hyundai Dealer’s inventory and has the remainder of the 60 months/60,000 miles New Vehicle Limited Warranty (NVLW). For such repairs, • Engine and transmission repairs will require Prior Approval. • Any used vehicle reconditioning, dents, scratches, or damage to a vehicle is not covered under any Hyundai Warranty. Note 1: HMA will monitor all in-stock vehicle warranty repairs. Note 2: It is the dealer’s responsibility to perform any open Service Campaigns or Recalls on used vehicles in dealer stock prior to selling or trading the vehicle. Service Campaigns or Recalls on used vehicles do not require Prior Approval Center authorization, unless otherwise stated.

title: Warranty Labor Rate Establishment
raw_content: The Dealer’s Warranty Labor Rate approved and reimbursed by HMA is intended to compensate the Dealer adequately, fairly and most importantly, competitively for warranty repairs performed. In determining fair, reasonable, and competitive warranty labor rates under this Section, areas of consideration include: • The Dealer’s current actual retail hourly labor rate. • The reasonableness of the requested labor rate in relation to local competition and market area. • The Dealer’s year-over-year labor rate increases.
coverage_and_reimbursement: Compensation for warranty repairs performed via the Dealer’s Warranty Labor Rate approved and reimbursed by HMA.

title: Warranty Labor Rate Request Procedure
raw_content: A warranty labor rate must be requested prior to the operation or start-up date. A change to an existing warranty labor rate may be requested under Subsection 5.10.3 after 12 months from the date of the most recent approved warranty labor rate. The following procedures must be adhered to when requesting a warranty labor rate under this Section. Please Note: The Dealer Principal or General Manager are the only individuals authorized to sign the Labor Rate Request Form under this Section. If the procedure is not adhered to, the Labor Rate Request form will be returned for corrections.

Request a change to an existing warranty labor rate under Subsection 5.10.3
Adhere to the procedures when requesting a warranty labor rate under this Section
Ensure the Labor Rate Request Form is signed by the Dealer Principal or General Manager

A change to an existing warranty labor rate may be requested under Subsection 5.10.3

title: New Dealer Labor Rate Establishment
raw_content: The procedures stated below must be followed for a new Dealer requesting the establishment of a Warranty Labor Rate under this Subsection: • Dealer obtains and DPSM/Region and/or Business Management Representative assists Dealer in completing a Warranty Labor Rate Request/Competitive Labor Rate Evaluation Form and in establishing a Warranty Labor Rate under this Subsection. This form must be submitted to the HMA Regional Office at least 30 days prior to dealership operation date. The form must include the Retail Labor Rate dealer is planning on charging the customer. This rate should be entered in the area called “Projected Posted Retail Customer Labor Rate” on the Labor Rate Evaluation Form. The Warranty Labor Rate Request form is available on www. HyundaiDealer.com under the Service Tab / Dealer Resources / Documents Library / Warranty. • Warranty Labor Rate Request /Competitive Labor Rate Evaluation form is submitted to the Regional Office for review/approval. Do not send the form directly to the National Warranty Department. *Competitor labor rates from dealers under the same ownership group as the requesting Hyundai dealer is not allowed. • Regional office will forward the reviewed form and proposed recommended rate to the National Warranty Department for approval and processing. National Warranty will not approve a region recommended rate that is greater than the Dealer’s “Projected Posted Retail Customer Labor Rate”. • Dealer receives notification of approval/disapproval of Warranty Labor Rate request from Regional office. Note: Allow at least 30 days for approval/disapproval of the requested Warranty Labor Rate from when the packet is received at National. Please note that the new dealer’s labor rate will be made effective (if approved by National) when the dealer becomes activated by Hyundai National’s Dealer Development. • DPSM/Regional Office may perform a review of at least 25 of the most current sequentially numbered Hyundai customer pay retail repair orders within 60 days after Dealer’s operation date to validate that the approved Warranty Labor Rate is not actually greater than the Dealer’s actual customer pay retail repair labor rate. If the DPSM/Regional Office verifies that the Dealer’s approved Warranty Labor Rate is greater than the Dealer’s actual customer pay retail repair labor rate during this review, HMA shall have the right to adjust the Dealer’s approved Warranty Labor Rate to reflect the Dealer’s actual customer pay retail repair labor rate.
