title: Existing Dealer Labor Rate Change
raw_content: The procedures stated below must be followed for an existing Dealer requesting a change in their Warranty Labor Rate under this Subsection. Labor rate increase requests under this Subsection will be approved up to a maximum of 5% less than the average RO labor dollars per hour calculated in the 25 RO survey. On a case-by-case basis, the actual labor rate approved by HMA may vary dependent on local market conditions and the total year-over- year increase being requested. Please note, Warranty Labor Rate requests submitted under this Subsection do not constitute labor rate requests under applicable state law procedures. Nothing in this policy prohibits a dealer from requesting to have its rate set under applicable state law procedures. The procedures stated below must be followed for an existing Dealer requesting a change in their Warranty Labor Rate under this Subsection: • Dealer provides and completes a Warranty Labor Rate Request/Competitive Labor Rate Evaluation Form*, Warranty Labor Rate Survey, a Warranty Labor Rate Checklist, and 25 most current sequentially numbered qualified Hyundai customer pay Retail Repair Orders (ROs). To be a qualified RO, the ROs must meet guidelines outlined in Section 5.10.6. to its regional office for review. *Competitor labor rates from dealers under the same ownership group as the requesting Hyundai dealer is not allowed. The above three forms can be found on www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service Tab / Dealer Resources / Documents Library / Warranty. Please be sure to highlight all qualifying repair lines (total labor hours and labor dollars). If the hours are not on the RO, please submit supporting documentation (such as a timesheet) with hours listed on it. HMA Dealer will provide to DPSM the Warranty Labor Rate Request Form, Warranty Labor Rate Survey, and 25 of its most current sequentially numbered qualified Hyundai customer pay RO s for review. The DPSM will validate and review the highlighted labor hour(s) and labor dollar(s) on each RO against the survey form. The DPSM should notify its regional office once it receives the dealer’s submission. Once DPSM validates the dealer’s submission, DPSM will forward to its regional office for review. If DPSM finds error(s) in dealer’s submission, it may return submission to dealer for correction. Please note that the submission can be returned to the dealer for the following reasons: • If ROs are not listed in sequential order on the Warranty Labor Rate Survey Form • If the supplied ROs are not in sequential order when provided for review • If the Warranty Labor Rate Request/Warranty Labor Rate Survey are not typed • If the ROs do not meet the guidelines outlined in Section 5.10.6 or include non- qualified ROs • If the ROs do not substantiate the dealer’s requested rate • Please do not include maintenance or special pricing repairs (see section 5.10.6 for further details on exclusions) and hours should be typed, not handwritten on the RO. • Once the submission is complete and reviewed by the DPSM, it will be forwarded to its regional office (Regional Warranty Manager) for review. If the regional office finds error(s) in dealer’s submission, it may return submission to DPSM for dealer to correct. • Once the regional office has reviewed the Dealer’s submission, the regional office will forward approved forms (Warranty Labor Rate Request Form, Warranty Labor Rate Survey, ROs, along with Check list) along with its proposed recommendation to the National Warranty Department for final approval and processing. Once the National Warranty Department reaches its decision, it will notify the Regional Office . Note: As set forth above, request for Warranty Labor Rates submitted under this Subsection do not constitute requests under state law and are not governed by state law. Nothing in this Subsection prohibits a Dealer to submit a request to increase its Warranty Labor Rate under applicable state law. Requests for Warranty Labor Rates submitted under state statute shall be governed by the applicable state law.

title: Warranty Labor Rate Request Form
raw_content: Available on www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service tab / Dealer Resources / Documents Library / Warranty / Warranty Labor Rate Request

title: Warranty Labor Rate Verification
raw_content: Approval of the requested Warranty Labor Rate is based on the following requirements and verification: • The customer pay retail labor rate that is established and implemented by the dealership must be reasonable and consistent with the local trade practices in the market area in which the dealership performs service business. The rate must be consistent with competition and generally accepted sound business practices. • The repair orders listed on the Warranty Labor Rate Evaluation Sheet must support the region’s recommended rate and not above the dealer’s posted retail rate for the customer. • The repair orders listed on the Warranty Labor Rate Evaluation Sheet must be in sequential order and the RO open date (not the RO closed date) is to be used in the RO open date column. • The customer pay retail labor rate charged to the retail customer must: 1. Be displayed and visible to the customer in both the service customer write- up/reception area and the service customer cashier area. 2. The lettering used to display the customer retail labor rate must be at least 2 inches high. 3. The rate must be displayed at all times during service operation and business hours and should state one or more of the following based upon the method or rate charged: • If the actual technician clock time to perform the repair is the method of labor charge, the words displayed should state: Retail Customer Labor Rate charges are based on a labor rate of $ _______ per hour. • If a published flat rate manual is used as the method of labor charge for the type of repairs, the words displayed should state: Retail customer labor rate charges are based on a labor rate of $ _______ per mechanical hour. Retail customer labor rate charges are based on a labor rate of $ _______ per body repair hour. Note: When the customer retail labor rate charge is based upon a published flat rate manual, the display of the posted rate must clearly state that “the dealership uses the labor time published in the (name of manual) labor time manual. The time published in the (name of manual) lists the average time required to perform the repair and therefore, since the time listed is an average time, the listed time may be more or less than the actual time spent to perform the repair” (consult with local laws for other requirements). • If variable or discounted rates are charged, the words displayed should state for example: Retail customer labor rate charges for Senior Citizens are based on a labor rate of $ ________ per hour. Note: It is advisable to state who qualifies or the criteria for the variable or discounted rate. • If a skill level, specialized, staggered or shop-track labor system is used as the method of labor charge, the words displayed should state: Retail customer labor rate charges are based on a labor rate of: $ ______ per hour for technician skill level A $ ______ per hour for technician skill level B $ ______ per hour for technician skill level C Note: Types of repairs must be correlated with the appropriate technician skill level. This information must be made readily available to the customer if requested by the customer. • If a team system is used, the displayed labor rate must state the fact that a team system is used by the dealership. • The retail customer labor rate must be pre-printed or written on all copies of the retail customer pay repair order. • The labor time charged to each retail customer must be stated in number of hours in tenths on the accounting copy of the retail customer pay repair order. Do not handwrite the hours on the Repair Order. • The labor charges for all retail customer repairs must be based on a labor time and rate that is consistent with the dealership’s practice. • The dealership must maintain a reasonable percentage of warranty service labor sales to total customer service and warranty service labor sales as determined by HMA. • If the regional office’s review of the 25 sequentially numbered Hyundai retail customer pay repair orders reveals any discrepancies or any of the above requirements are not adhered to, the difference between the new verified rate and the current warranty labor rate will be charged back to the dealer for all labor hours approved under the unsubstantiated warranty labor rate. The new verified warranty labor rate will become the Dealer’s approved warranty labor rate.

Ensure the repair orders listed on the Warranty Labor Rate Evaluation Sheet support the region’s recommended rate and are not above the dealer’s posted retail rate for the customer.

Display specific wording about labor charge method based on actual technician clock time, a published flat rate manual, variable/discounted rates, a skill level/specialized/staggered/shop-track labor system, or a team system.

When using a published flat rate manual, clearly state that “the dealership uses the labor time published in the (name of manual) labor time manual. The time published in the (name of manual) lists the average time required to perform the repair and therefore, since the time listed is an average time, the listed time may be more or less than the actual time spent to perform the repair”.
Correlate types of repairs with the appropriate technician skill level.
Pre-print or write the retail customer labor rate on all copies of the retail customer pay repair order.
State the labor time charged to each retail customer in number of hours in tenths on the accounting copy of the retail customer pay repair order.
Do not handwrite the hours on the Repair Order.
Ensure labor charges for all retail customer repairs are based on a labor time and rate that is consistent with the dealership’s practice.
Maintain a reasonable percentage of warranty service labor sales to total customer service and warranty service labor sales as determined by HMA.

title: Retail Customer Repair Order Evaluation and Exclusions
raw_content: Upon submission of a Warranty Labor Rate Request form to the Regional Office under Subsections 5.10.2 or 5.10.3, the DPSM/Regional office may perform, evaluate and verify at least 25 sequentially numbered Hyundai retail customer pay repair orders in order to verify that the labor rate actually charged to retail customers agrees with the dealer’s retail customer labor rate posted. Hyundai Motor America will only accept properly documented retail customer paid repair orders, provided they follow the procedure described below. All repair order submissions must meet the guidelines described above or will be subject to denial. The 25 sequentially numbered Hyundai repair orders (ROs) should consist of the most recent retail customer pay repair orders and be in sequential order. ALL customer repair line(s) on each RO must display the labor hour(s) and labor dollar(s) on the RO or provide qualifying back up (such as a timesheet). Repair Order (RO) Requirements: The repair order provided must contain the following information at a minimum: I. Dealer Logo/Name and Address II. Repair Order must indicate “Accounting, Service File Copy, or Customer Receipt Copy” on the actual RO *IF a dealer uses an electronic repair order system, the dealer must provide the “Accounting, Service File Copy, or Customer Receipt Copy” in addition to the initial repair order and final invoice copy) III. The Totals Section should display the following: Total Labor, Total Parts, Total Sales Tax, Total Charges and Discounts, Miscellaneous, or Less Insurance IV. The RO must include the following information: a. RO number b. VIN c. RO open and close dates (close date is the date the vehicle leaves dealership- document if customer doesn’t pick up vehicle when first informed that it is ready) d. Vehicle mileage (in and out) e. Owner/ Customer’s name, address, and phone number f. Description of customer’s complaint, cause and correction g. Customer’s signature for initial repair order write up h. Customer’s signature for receipt of repair completed vehicle (pre-invoice, customer, accounting, final invoice) i. Replacement part number j. Part(s) dollar amount (cost and part amount charged to the retail customer) k. Part quantity l. Labor dollar amount (if applicable) m. Labor hours (if applicable) n. Technician comments o. Display and indicate any discounts offered to the retail customer p. Summary of the “Totals Section” (parts, labor, tax, and etc. / A description of all payments received. Retail Customer Pay Repair Exclusions: The type of repairs listed below will not be part of the evaluation. · Discounts (i.e. repairs with discounts) · All factory recommended maintenance services such as 7.5K, 15K, 22.5K, 30K, engine oil, air/oil filters belts (timing, serpentine, V-Belt, drive), spark plugs, tune-ups, fluid flushes & refills, etc. · Internal, extended warranty, extended service contract, insurance company, or similar paid repairs. · Any repairs that are not fully customer paid · Dealership Goodwill repairs, including repairs where the Dealer absorbs a portion of the repair costs. · Repairs due to customer abuse · Brake pads (unless part of a larger repair such as replacement of rotors) · Air Conditioner (AC) Refrigerant Evacuation and Recharge · Wheel balance and alignment · Tire service such as replacement and rotation. · Vehicle Reconditioning · Wiper blades · Any repairs with Non-Hyundai make vehicles · State safety and smog inspections · All service specials * · Any menu related repairs · Variables rates applied to specific fleets. * Note: Service specials are generally defined as advertised sales, which may be in effect for a short period or are ongoing. Certain ongoing discounted repair items (e.g. menu repairs) are not considered service specials. Note: The list above is not all-inclusive and is subject to change. For Customer Pay (CP) repair orders, be sure to highlight all qualifying repair lines (i.e., repairs that do not fall under the list of the above retail customer repair exclusions) . For example, if there is a CP repair order that includes the following repairs and times, highlight each qualifying line and SUM THE TOTAL HOURS AND LABOR DOLLARS. The sums for the repair order will be entered on one line of the Warranty Labor Rate Survey. RO#12345A Line 1 Replace alternator 1.0 hours $150.00 Line 2 Replace compressor 1.5 hours $225.00 Line 3 Replace TPMS 0.5 hours $75.00 Line 4 Change oil and filter 0.4 hours $60.00 Total Qualifying Repair Lines 3.0 hours $450.00

title: Warranty Claim Corrections
raw_content: In instances where an approved/paid warranty claim has been underpaid, overpaid, or contained incorrect or incomplete information such as part number(s), part quantities, labor operation code(s), labor quantities, sublet, towing freight, mileage, etc. Dealer can self-correct the claim two separate ways, depending on if the Total Cost will be impacted. For any approved/paid claims needing an adjustment that will change the claim amount, Dealer may chargeback the original claim and create a new corrected claim with a new claim number. Do not adjust the original claim, or your resubmission will be denied. For any approved/paid claims needing a non-financial adjustment (i.e. mileage discrepancy, etc.), Dealer may chargeback, adjust, and resubmit the claim (same claim number). The following claims will need to be charged back by the Warranty Call Center 1-877-446-2922: • Any claim(s) that are over the immediate Friday since the payment date. • Any claim(s) that have been paid incorrectly, and your DCS Debit screen is not working.

Do not adjust the original claim if the adjustment will change the claim amount.
Chargeback, adjust, and resubmit the claim (using the same claim number) for non-financial adjustments.
Contact the Warranty Call Center at 1-877-446-2922 for claims over the immediate Friday since the payment date or for incorrectly paid claims when the DCS Debit screen is not working.

title: Foreign Vehicle Claims
raw_content: Claims for foreign vehicles being serviced and operated in the United States should be submitted using the Foreign Vehicle Warranty Claim screen located in Hyundai WebDCS under the Warranty tab. Go to WEBDCS > Warranty Tab > Information > Foreign Vehicle Information screen to obtain the production and in-service dates of the foreign VIN. To verify warranty coverage or for technical support on a foreign vehicle, please be sure to contact the country of origin prior to performing any repairs. The contact information for the countries of origin can be found on the Foreign Vehicle Warranty Coverage Guide and Contact Information. Once repairs are completed, submit the claim along with attachments required for digital documentation, repair order, and technician copy in WebDCS Foreign Vehicle Warranty Claim screen. Submit the applicable SRC or 3 rd party rental in the Rental Claim screen. For any questions relating to foreign vehicle claims, please submit inquiries by using the Warranty Support Portal in WebDCS.

title: Dealer Responsibility for Customer Satisfaction
raw_content: Repeat visits for the same issue : Must contact Hyundai Techline (800) 325-6604 for assistance when having trouble in diagnosing, identifying, or repairing a customer's complaint before beginning the Second, Third, Fourth, or greater repair attempt for the same problem. Document the contact date, time, and case number on the repair order. Days out of service : If a vehicle is down for over 10 days for parts backorder or technical issues, Dealer must contact the DPSM. If the parts necessary for the repair are on back order and do not affect either the (1) drivability of the vehicle or (2) the safety of the vehicle, Dealer shall return the vehicle to the customer and close out the repair order while the parts are on order. Any parts needed to repair an inoperative Hyundai vehicle must be ordered as an “Emergency Order” and upgraded to “XVOR” if the part is on back order. Customer Satisfaction : Notify the DPSM and Regional Consumer Affairs Manager whenever experiencing an issue with customer satisfaction due to warrantable repairs or days out of service. Refer the customer to Hyundai Consumer Affairs line 1-800-633-5151 and the BBB Auto Line 1-800-955-5100.

title: Sublet Repair Requirements
raw_content: Sublet repairs are an important part of dealer operations but carry additional responsibilities to maintain compliance with safety regulations and customer satisfaction expectations. This policy outlines the requirements for sublet repairs. These requirements are designed to protect the integrity of repairs and ensure that all work performed meets OE specifications. Below are common sublet repairs and sublet repair requirements. Paint/Anti-Perforation To ensure high-quality repairs, compliance with environmental and safety regulations and to maintain consistent industry standards, dealers should not engage or authorize mobile paint operations for any paint repair, refinishing work or body panel repairs. All paint and body related repairs must be performed at a fixed, licensed facility that meets local, state and federal regulations. Repairs must be completed in a controlled environment to ensure proper curing, finish quality, and adherence to OE specifications. All invoices must be typed and printed. Handwritten invoices shall not be allowed for repair transactions. This requirement ensures legibility, accuracy and documentation compliance. Sublet vendors must use an electronic invoicing system or a computer-generated format. Invoices must include: • Vendor name, address and contact information • Detailed description of services performed • Itemized costs for each services performed • Date of service Glass Dealers must ensure that all sublet glass installations are performed by qualified vendors in compliance with OE specifications and safety standards. If a windshield or other glass component is found damaged or compromised due to improper installation by a sublet glass vendor, the sublet vendor shall be fully responsible for repairing any damage caused by the improper installation, providing and installing a replacement windshield or glass component at no additional cost, and responsible for any related labor and materials necessary to restore the vehicle to OE standards. Cylinder Head Inspection All invoices must include: • Vendor name, address and contact information • Detailed description of services performed • Itemized costs for each services performed • Date of service Failure to comply with Sublet Repair Requirements may result in denial of claim approval or claim being subject to chargeback. Dealer are responsible for ensuring that all sublet operations adhere to the requirements outlined. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 130 Section 6: .0 Dealership Documentation Requirements The information supplied on the repair order, warranty claim form, and other supporting documents is intended to substantiate the Dealer’s claim for payment. In addition, this information assists the Manufacturer in determining which parts are failing, why they are failing, and provides information for evaluation of the reliability of the product. It is the Dealer’s responsibility to ensure that every claim submitted contains complete, accurate information, that the claim is fully and correctly executed, and that the supporting documentation, including the repair order, is legible and understandable. This policy also applies to other claim types submitted to HMA for payment; including Campaign, PDI, Goodwill, and Retail Part. It is also the Dealer’s responsibility to submit the claim within the required time interval. All warranty claims must be submitted to HMA within 30 days of the repair order close date unless state law provides otherwise. The repair order close date is the date when the vehicle repairs have been completed and should similarly coincide with the date the customer picks up their vehicle. All warranty claims returned to a Dealer as incomplete or incorrect must also be resubmitted to HMA within 30 days from the date the claim was returned to Dealer. Failure to fully and accurately complete and submit the claim will be sufficient grounds for rejection or chargeback of the claim. The record retention and submission requirements stated in the following articles apply to the submission of all types of claims submitted to HMA for payment, including warranty, Campaign, PDI, Goodwill, and Retail Part. The requirements apply to both traditional manual repair order systems and electronic repair order systems unless specifically noted otherwise. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 131

title: Repair Order and Warranty Claim Submission Requirements
raw_content: The repair order is a document that is generated in response to a customer complaint or request. Occasionally, a request indicates only the need for an explanation of a certain feature or the proper operation of a particular component or accessory. In these cases it may not always be necessary to generate a repair order. A repair order must be written or computer generated when: • An inspection of the vehicle is required. • A repair, replacement, or adjustment is to be performed (including sublet or outside repairs). • A Pre-Delivery Inspection service is to be performed. • A customer statement needs to be documented for future reference. • A campaign (recall) or owner notification program repair or adjustment is to be performed. • Only one diagnostic labor operation (RQ0) is allowed for each repair order (RO). A properly written repair order will: • Describe work in complaint, cause, and correction (3C) format. • Accurately record the customer’s complaint as stated by the customer. • Communicate to the technician either the specific symptoms that the vehicle is exhibiting or a customer complaint for further diagnosis. • Document technician verification of the complaint and indicate the cause of the complaint. • Document the corrective action used to resolve the complaint. • Complete the Technician Comments section on every repair order. • Substantiate the warranty repair in compliance with HMA’s warranty stipulations. Because every warranty claim has its origin in a repair order, a complete and legible repair order will ensure: • Faster warranty payment • Fewer returned claims • Reduced administrative workload The Dealership’s customer service file and the Hyundai WebDCS Warranty Vehicle Information screen must always be checked during the repair order write-up process. A printout of the Warranty Vehicle Information screen must be attached to the service file copy of the RO. This includes dealerships which have an electronic service file as well. This ensures that prior repairs to the same or similar component are recognized. Repeat repairs due to poor diagnosis or improper repair are not covered under warranty. It is the responsibility of the dealership to stand behind its workmanship. Shop comebacks are not acceptable under warranty. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 132 Checking and attaching a copy of the Hyundai WebDCS Warranty Vehicle Information screen will ensure that the Dealer is aware of any outstanding campaigns on the vehicle as well as any applicable HPP or CPO status on the vehicle. Hyundai Motor America does not require the utilization of a specific repair order form or that the format be organized in a specifically defined manner. HMA does however stipulate that the repair order form utilized be clear and legible, contain specific details of information, and be administered as follows: 1. The repair order form utilized for warranty claim submission must contain at least the four following copies: • Dealer Accounting File Copy (Initial repair order and final invoice copies for electronic repair orders) • Service File Copy or Parts Scrapping Copy • Shop Hard Copy (Technician Input) • Customer Receipt Copy (Initial repair order and final invoice copies for electronic repair orders) In certain states or local jurisdictions, additional requirements may be stipulated. Dealers should contact their legal counsel for clarification on this issue. Carbonless or transferable areas on the repair order form ensure the transfer of appropriate information to certain copies only. It is appropriate that repair order copies contain only pertinent information. The Dealer must retain the repair order copies in the appropriate area. Accounting copy files must be kept in numerical sequence in the business office. Customer service file copies and technician input shop hard copies must be retained in the service department organized by vehicle identification number (VIN) in numerical sequence. If an electronic repair order system is used, copies of the initial customer write-up repair order must be retained with the accounting files along with a copy of the final invoice. Electronic facsimile paper copies of the final repair order must also be retained in the customer service file in the service department. The Dealer must provide each owner for whom warranty or customer satisfaction service is performed with a completed copy of the repair order stating all repairs and services performed. Certain states or local jurisdictions stipulate that a customer be provided an estimate copy at the time of original write-up. Dealers should contact their legal counsel for clarification on this issue. Note: In dealerships which utilize an electronic repair order system, the Customer must be provided a copy of both the original initial write-up repair order and the final invoice. Likewise, Customer service file copies of both the original initial write-up repair order and the final Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 133 invoice, including all parts replaced, must be retained in the service department, organized by vehicle identification number (VIN) in numerical sequence. 2. Repair orders issued to perform a Pre-Delivery Inspection must include the following minimum information: • Service Manager’s clear and legible signature • Dealer Logo and Address • Repair Order Number • Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) • Vehicle mileage before and after the required test drive • Technician time recording - See section 6.3 for requirements • Repair Order Date • Purchaser’s name, address and telephone number – If the vehicle has not been sold by the date of performance of the Pre-Delivery Inspection, use the dealer’s assigned vehicle stock number in place of purchaser’s information. 3. Repair orders issued to perform warranty, Campaign, or Goodwill repairs must include the following minimum information: • Repair Order Number • Dealer Logo and Address • Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) • Vehicle Mileage - In and Out – HMA’s policy is to recalibrate the new speedometer with the vehicle mileage at the time of failure replacement. In cases where dealer is unable to locate a vendor to recalibrate the new speedometer, then the repaired/replaced odometer should be adjusted to read 0 miles and a Speedometer/Odometer Repair/Replacement Sticker should be affixed to the driver side door frame indicating that such a repair was performed. These speedometer stickers are available from HMA‘s Non-Parts Warehouse, part #00924-00000. If a vehicle comes into your dealership for service which has a Speedometer/Odometer Repair/Replacement Sticker on the door frame, please add the mileages of the odometer and sticker to obtain the true mileage of the vehicle. State and local laws and regulations for this repair should also be reviewed to determine if there are additional obligations which might apply. • Repair Order Open Date and Close Date (close date is the date the vehicle leaves the dealership-document if customer doesn’t pick up vehicle when first informed that it is ready) • Vehicle Delivery Date or Date of First Use • Owner/Customer’s name, address and telephone number • Complaint - Description of the customer’s complaint as stated by the customer (the repair order should state words such as “customer states” followed by customer’s complaint) Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 134 • Cause - Description of cause of complaint • Correction - Description of the actions taken by the technician to correct the complaint • Customer’s signature for initial repair order write-up • Customer’s signature for receipt of repair completed vehicle • Claim Number(s) • Prior approval number authorized by the Prior Approval Center (if applicable) • Nature Code and Explanation • Cause Code and Explanation • Labor Dollar Amount (if applicable) • Labor Operation Code(s), Quantities and Hours (if applicable) • Part(s) Dollar Amount (if applicable) • Part(s) Quantity (if applicable) • Causal Part Number • Replacement Part Numbers (if applicable) • Sublet Amount and Sublet Code(s) (if applicable) • Sublet Final Invoice Number and Name (if applicable) • Towing Amount (if applicable) • Towing Invoice Number and Name (if applicable) • Freight Amount (if applicable) • Freight Carrier (if applicable) • Freight Shipping Date (if applicable) • For Replacement Service Parts: the date of previous, original installation; previous, original mileage, and previous, original repair order or counter sales ticket number (if applicable) • Technician Comments 4. The repair order’s technician input/shop hard copy must be separated from the repair order’s remaining soft copies prior to the work being performed. The technician is to be given the technician input/ shop hard copy of the repair order as the technician’s work copy at the time of repair performance. The remaining repair order soft copies are to be sent to the Parts Department or elsewhere as needed. The complete repair order, including the technician input/shop hard copy and all the remaining soft copies are to be sent to the appropriate area for close out of the repair order after completion of the repair. 5. The repair order must contain all parts replaced as per the repair. The parts listed must be legible and contain: • Complete Replacement Part Number(s) • Replacement Part Quantity(s) issued • Dealer Net cost of replacement part(s) on the date the repair is performed. (The extended total Dealer net costs must be stated for replacement part quantities greater than 1) Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 135 If an automated parts invoice or a separate parts invoice is used, the automated or separate parts invoice must contain cross-reference to the repair order and be attached to the technician input/shop hard copy that is retained in the customer/VIN service file. The repair order must also contain cross-reference to the automated or separate parts invoice. 6. The customer must sign and approve all repairs for complaints stated on the repair order. The customer must sign the repair order prior to the work being performed and upon receipt of the vehicle after completion of the repairs. Dealerships which utilize an electronic repair order system must ensure that a customer signature is obtained on both the original initial write-up repair order copy as well as the final invoice copy. Digital signatures, or other electronic acknowledgements (E.g., signatures on a tablet, approval via text, email, or other app), are acceptable means of capturing customer authorizations. Electronic acknowledgements should be dated and time stamped, printed, attached, and retained as part of the work order. If the customer uses a night drop envelope, the night drop envelope must contain the customer’s signature and should contain the date of drop off. The signed night drop envelope must be retained in the service files attached to the technician input/shop hard copy of the repair order. In the event that the Dealer cannot obtain a customer signature on a night drop envelope of a repair order, the Service Manager must sign and date the repair order. The Service Manager must sign the repair order for vehicles repaired that are in stock and for the repair orders issued to perform the Pre-Delivery Inspection. Digital signatures (including the ones captured via tablet) are acceptable means of capturing the Service Manager’s signature. Upon completion of the repairs and receipt of the vehicle, the customer must receive the customer receipt copy of the completed repair order which must contain a description of the warranty repairs performed and the parts replaced on the vehicle. 7. The technician input/shop hard copy or electronic hard copy of the repair order must contain the technician’s comments and description of the repair the technician performed. The description written by the technician must include the cause of the customer’s complaint and include notes describing the diagnostic process. The technician must also document the corrective action used to resolve the complaint. In addition, technical measurements or readings that are required in order to determine the serviceability or performance of a part or assembly, as stated in the applicable shop manuals, must be stated on the technician input shop hard copy/electronic hard copy of the repair order. Repairs should follow shop manuals procedures including replacing one-time use parts. One- time use parts designated in order to perform the repair need to be charged out on the warranty repair order in order complete the repair properly. If a warranty repair requires one-time use parts to properly repair a customer vehicle and the warranty claim is submitted without the one- time use parts, the warranty claim may be subject to denial or chargeback. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 136 Ensure that all repairs are carried out in accordance with the latest published procedures detailed in shop manuals or other service related publications such as Technical Service Bulletins and campaigns. Refer to the most recent model and model year specific repair manual Technical Service Bulletin for diagnostic and available repair details. Before and after readings/measurements must be recorded and documented. Parts requiring measurements and readings include, but are not limited to: • Air conditioning Evac & Recharge refrigerant • Automatic transmission oil pressure test/Diagnosis Worksheet • ATM Tester • Battery tester readings/Battery code and printout • Brake discs/rotors • Brake shoes • Idle speed/Throttle body • Cylinder compression • Cylinder leak down • Emission test readings • Engine Diagnosis Worksheet • GDS test printouts/readings • Gear box preload • Headlamp aiming • Ignition timing • Oil consumption test • Test drive results • Valve clearances • Vehicle Tracking Data Sheet • Wheel alignment, including Tire Balance Data Sheet • Wheel balance Note: Go to www.HyundaiDealer.com under the Service tab/Dealer Resources/Documents Library/ Warranty for the latest Test and Measurement Documentation Chart Dealerships which utilize an electronic repair order must adhere to the same requirements, and technicians must input their comments into a computer terminal. In all instances where technician’s comments are updated, a hard copy of the repair order with the technician’s comments must be generated and retained in the customer/VIN service file. This requirement also applies to repairs orders when information is entered using a digital service lane technology application. 8. The individual technician performing the repair must be identified by name or number on the face of the technician input shop hard copy of the repair order next to each labor operation the Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 137 technician performed. Technician identification documented for a repair must be true and accurate. This requirement also applies to dealerships which utilize a team system. Identification by team only is not adequate. A code or name must also be utilized with an electronic repair order system. 9. All information added to the technician input shop hard copy of the repair order after the initial repair order write-up with the customer, must be transferred and added to the remaining repair order soft copies, including the customer receipt copy. Subsequent generations of a given repair order in an electronic repair order system must be printed and retained in the customer/VIN service file such that the evolution of a repair order can be demonstrated. 10. Warranty repairs made without a customer complaint are add-on repairs, and are only allowed under these circumstances: • For completion of applicable campaigns or recalls. • For repairs to items that would seriously impact the operational safety of the vehicle (for example SRS light, ABS light, Check Engine Light, or profuse fluid leaks). Add-on Warranty repairs must adhere to the following administration requirements: – The technician must provide a complete description of the needed work on the hard copy of the repair order. The description must include the cause, including notes describing the diagnosis. The technician must also document the corrective action. – The Service Manager must verify the needed repair and sign next to the added repair on the hard copy of the repair order. This authorization must be clearly documented next to the technician’s description of the problem prior to the work being performed. A Service Manager’s signature at the top or bottom of the invoice is not acceptable. If the customer on the repair order was notified of the add-on repair by phone, it is acceptable to document on the repair order hard copy the date, time, contact method and the name of the customer who authorized the add-on repair. The customer who authorized the add-on repair should be the same customer on the repair order. The Service Manager’s signature must be next to the add-on repair line. – The additional repair information must be transferred and added to all remaining copies of the repair order. – The customer’s signature must appear next to the additional repair that has been added to the repair order. The signature must be next to the added repair description. A customer’s signature at the top or bottom of the invoice is not acceptable. – If an electronic repair order system is used, the add-on repair must be indicated with some sort of identifier such as an “*”. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 138 If a warranty repair is generated from a customer pay repair order, whereby the dealer uses separate repair orders for customer pay and warranty, the repair is considered an add-on repair. These repairs are subject to the same requirements as repairs made without a customer complaint. The customer pay repair order must have these items: • The Service Manager’s signature next to the added repair(s) on the hard copy of the repair order. • The warranty repair description. • A cross-reference to the warranty repair order. Complaints added by the customer after the time of initial write-up are customer add-on repairs. The date and time the additional request was made must be documented on the repair order. The following authorization requirements apply: • The Service Manager must sign next to the added customer complaint before the repair work is performed. The signature must be next to the added repair description. A Service Manager’s signature at the top or bottom of the invoice is not acceptable. If the customer on the repair order was notified of the add-on repair by phone, it is acceptable to document on the repair order hard copy the date, time, contact method and the name of the customer who authorized the add-on repair. The customer who authorized the add-on repair should be the same customer on the repair order. The Service Manager’s signature must be next to the add-on repair line. 11. In certain cases, a repair cannot be completed due to replacement parts not being available. In these cases, do not submit the warranty claim for the diagnosis or repair until the part is installed and the repair completed. Drivable vehicles are to be returned to the customer while waiting for the part to arrive. When the vehicle is returned to the customer, fill in the “date/mileage in” and “date/mileage out” fields accurately on the repair order. On the repair order, be sure to add a statement that the vehicle is drivable and was returned while waiting for the parts to arrive. When the part arrives to the dealer: • Open a new repair order using the date and mileage of when the vehicle was originally presented for diagnosis or repair. • On the new repair order, refer to the original repair order number when the part was originally ordered and repeat the original customer complaint statement. • In the customer concern section of the new repair order, input the repair completion date and mileage. • In the customer concern section of the new repair order, include the following information: o The date and mileage when the vehicle was originally presented for diagnosis or repair. o The date the parts were ordered o The parts status at the time originally ordered (e.g. backordered, etc.). o The date the parts arrived at the dealer. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 139 o The date and mileage of when the repairs were completed. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 140

Repeat repairs due to poor diagnosis or improper repair are not covered under warranty.
Shop comebacks are not acceptable under warranty.

If a warranty repair requires one-time use parts to properly repair a customer vehicle and the warranty claim is submitted without the one-time use parts, the warranty claim may be subject to denial or chargeback.
Service Manager’s signature for add-on repairs is not acceptable if placed at the top or bottom of the invoice instead of next to the added repair.
Customer’s signature for add-on repairs is not acceptable if placed at the top or bottom of the invoice instead of next to the added repair.

title: Repair Digital Documentation
raw_content: Digital documentation of warranty, campaign, and goodwill repairs requires video or photo attachments of VIN, Miles, Part and Sublet(when applicable) to the claim. Dealer can use GDS/STUI or any electronic device to capture the required repair information, photos or videos, to attach to the claim. Using GDS/STUI can automatically capture vehicle’s VIN and Miles (2019MY+ only, for older models take photo of odometer), however, a photo must be uploaded to the Submission Process Tracker in GDS for the auto capture info to transfer to HMA. Photos and videos uploaded to HMA via STUI are available in WebDCS to attach to the claim. Photos, videos, and documents captured by the technician related to the repair must be attached to the claim in WebDCS. The photos and/or videos should be digital, colored, high- resolution, original, and unedited displaying the defective part and/or the area of concern. A category must be assigned to each attachment file. In cases where GDS/STUI is not available, such as an outage, dealer may take photos or videos using separate device and manually attach files to the claim. Multiple uses of the same photo image for Prior Approval submission is not allowed. If a warranty, campaign, or goodwill repair attaches the required digital documentation to substantiate the claim at submission, HMA will not require separate and distinguishable technician on/off punch times for any job that is identified through a labor operation in the Labor Time Standard, as well as any Campaign/Recall or op code in a TSB. It is the responsibility of the Dealer to verify that the required digital documentation is attached to the claim at the time the claim is submitted for payment. Straight Time (NTT, ATT), and claims (including Campaign/Recall) utilizing non-HMA LTS hours must still be supported by separate and distinguishable on/off punch times, see sections 5.8 and 6.3. Note: Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI) claims do not require technician punch times. For campaigns/recalls, please check the TSB instructions for specific STUI/digital documentation requirements. If not specified in TSB, then VIN and Miles are required. Document the repair or part replacement as instructed in the TSB. HMA reserves the right at any time in its sole discretion to require a dealer to submit punch times (see sections 5.8 and 6.3) instead of the digital documentation policy outlined above. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 141

title: GDS Basic Connection Requirements (BASICA00)
raw_content: Claim submission for GDS Basic Connection of operation code BASICA00 (0.1) applies to warranty, paint, anti-perforation, hybrid, emissions, and powertrain claim types. BASICA00 does not apply to other claims, such as campaign, retail part, or goodwill claim types. BASICA00 also does not apply without a related warranty repair claim. BASICA00 can only be submitted once per repair order. If the repair order contains a diagnostic RQ0 (0.3) operation code or No Trouble Found (NTF), BASICA00 will not apply for claim submission. All modules must be selected from the GDS Fault Code Searching (FCS) and the GDS results must be uploaded to HMA in order to qualify for BASICA00 submission. As mentioned in 6.2.1 Repair Order Retention Requirements, a GDS screenshot should be saved in the repair order documentation retention and the screenshot should be attached onto a claim if FCS GDS results cannot be uploaded to HMA. title: Repair Order Retention Requirements
raw_content: As stated in the Sales and Service Agreement, Dealers are required to maintain all warranty and policy repair orders and related documentation for a period of five years, exclusive of any retention period required by any governmental entity. The retention period begins with the date of claim payment, not from the date the repair was performed. Dealers must retain all substantiating records and documents for all claims submitted to HMA for payment. The supporting documents must be arranged in a systematic order and be readily accessible for HMA review. Note: Repair order copies requested by HMA must be submitted to HMA by the Dealer within ten business days from the date of the request. The following list of supporting records and documents must be available for examination by HMA’s representatives: • Technician input/shop hard copy, service file copy (both original initial write-up repair order and final invoice copies for electronic systems) and parts scrapping copy of the repair order. • Dealer accounting file copy of the repair order (This copy must list the part(s) replaced and must indicate the labor charge). • The DCS acknowledgment (Claim Status Report) copy (from the day after submission) and any supporting documents, properly cross-referenced, must be retained when a warranty claim is subsequently prepared and submitted to HMA. • Attached to the 10 year/100,000 mile Powertrain Repair Order and retained in the dealer files: A fully executed 10 year/100,000 mile Powertrain Limited Warranty Original Owner Verification Affidavit Form, along with the documents listed below. 1. A photocopy of the customer’s current and up-to-date (not expired) owner registration from Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) as required by Prior Approval Request Submission (5.4.2) for 10/100k Powertrain warranty validation. 2. A printout of the Warranty Vehicle Information Screen. • All detailed records used to prepare payroll for Dealer employees who performed the particular repairs or replacements (includes time ticket and flag sheet hard copies). • Original or true copies of final invoices covering parts or services purchased outside, and the record of payment of such invoices, (e.g. sublet repair final invoices, purchase orders, accounts payable files, parts inventory files, open account statements, etc.) along with Rental Agreements and other relevant documents • New vehicle invoices • Customer sales files (includes copy of Retail Delivery Registration (RDR) form, customer sales contract, etc.) Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 143 • Customer service files (includes Pre-Delivery Checklist, Vehicle Delivery Presentation, and copies of all repair orders including warranty, service campaign, Dealer internals, customer pay, body shop estimates, in-transit damage, etc.) • Warranty Claim Register • Dealer Financial Statements • Loading/Distribution Sheets • GDS machine before and after readings – upon completion of any test, take screenshot using GDS screen capture function for attachment to claim • Any other records used by the Dealer to support the information recorded on the claim form These requirements fully apply to electronic repair order systems as well as traditional manual repair order generation systems.

Retain all substantiating records and documents for all claims submitted to HMA for payment

title: Electronic Document Storage Requirements
raw_content: Hyundai supports the use of fully integrated document retention systems that meet or exceed the following requirements. All documentation must: • Be complete and retained as outlined in 6.2.1 Repair Order Retention Requirements in effect at the time of the repair. • Be clearly legible for review and available upon request. • Be easily retrievable by VIN sequence. • Be backed up to a separate storage drive, providing redundancy for all customer and vehicle data. Original documents may be discarded only if there is a way to retrieve acceptable electronically stored documents that are identical copies of the original, and the dealer must have a back-up recovery plan to replace electronically stored documents in the event of disaster. Document storage systems must: • Prohibit changes to the document after it has been imaged. If modification is necessary, both original and modified copies must be available, with complete explanation to include the name of the individual who approved the RO modification and date of modification. • Protects against information being altered or destroyed once a repair order is finally closed. Note: Any electronic communication (E.g., text messages, email, social media posts) done between the dealer and customer regarding any form of repair approval or update must be time stamped and stored in a manner that cannot be altered and can be retrieved in case of future audit or other business needs of HMA. Digital signatures, or other electronic acknowledgements Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 144 (E.g., signatures on a tablet, approval via text/email, or other apps), are acceptable means of capturing customer authorizations and must also be dated and stored in a manner that cannot be altered and can be retrieved in case of future audit or other business need of HMA. In the event that a dealer is unable to produce sufficient RO documentation upon request or required control features are not being utilized, HMA reserves the right to chargeback the subject claims. Dealerships may utilize an electronic document storage system that meets the above requirements to retain all required documentation for the 5 year total retention period as required per the HMA Dealer Sales and Service Agreement. In certain states or local jurisdictions, additional requirements may be stipulated. Dealers should contact their legal counsel for clarification on this issue. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 145

title: Time Recording Requirements
raw_content: The actual starting and ending times for all warranty repairs must be documented using one of these methods: • A time punch clock which must show month, day, and time. • An electronic repair order (ERO) system that records the times automatically on the service file or invoice copy of the repair order. If a time punch clock is used, technicians must record their actual beginning and ending times by punching on and off of a Daily Time Ticket (flag sheet). Customer pay and internal work performed on the same repair order with warranty work requires separate time recording. The information on each time flag must be completely filled out. This includes the repair order number, technician identification, the type of repair performed (customer pay, internal, or warranty), labor time standard hours, and the appropriate line on the repair order that corresponds with the repair performed. The time flag adhesive strips must be removed from the flag sheet and affixed to the back of the repair order hard copy. The carbon copy of the flag sheet must be retained by the dealership for a period of five years, exclusive of any retention period required by any governmental entity. HMA reserves the right to chargeback any claim where the Dealer failed to retain the carbon copy of the flag sheet. HMA will accept computerized clocking entries from an electronic repair order system provided they reflect “ON” and “OFF” entries. The actual system generated technician time tracking must be printed, and appear on the service file or invoice copy of the repair order. Time punch clocks are required when utilizing an electronic repair order system that does not generate “ON” and “OFF” clocking substantiation. Actual labor time operations (ATT and NTT) not listed in the Labor Time Standard Manual that are listed on claims submitted to HMA for reimbursement must comply with the following requirements: • The technician must provide a detailed explanation of why actual time is needed on the hard copy of the repair order. Technician comments should be entered in the claim in the ‘Additional comments’ field. If Techline was contacted, the Techline Case # should also be included. • Service Manager must review all actual time diagnosis and repairs. The Service Manager must consent to the actual time next to the repair line by: • Signing his or her name • Printing his or her name • Noting the time consented A Service Manager’s signature at the top or bottom of the invoice is not acceptable. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 146 • A separate time recording is required to document the actual time spent by the technician. Each actual time operation requires separate beginning and ending time recording. • For diagnostic actual time labor operations, an explanation of the applicable diagnostic equipment and method must be documented on the hard copy of the repair order. The documentation must also include before and after readings/measurements. The general requirements shown below are to be followed for all time operations by all Dealers on claims submitted to HMA. • The technician’s time may not be recorded on two or more repairs at the same time. Overlapping time recorded on two or more repairs will be subject to chargeback. To avoid the possibility of overlapping time recording, no more than one repair order may be assigned to a technician at any given time. • If the technician inadvertently forgets to punch the beginning or ending time for a given repair, the Service Manager may handwrite the missing time punch. The Service Manager must authorize the time recording by recording the date and signing next to the flag strip affixed to the hard copy of the repair order. • Only authorized members of service management who are non-production/working technicians may enter handwritten time. A member of service management who also performs warranty repairs and is separately paid for such work is not authorized to sign time clocking. In this case, the Dealership General Manager must sign the handwritten time and actual time repairs. • When more than one technician performs repairs on the same vehicle, the time for each technician (including team lead technicians) must be recorded separately. The time flag for each technician must be affixed to the back of the repair order hard copy. • Starting and ending times for PDI repair orders must be accounted for separately from time spent installing accessories or completing other repairs. The Service Manager must authorize any time spent on repairs for vehicles during the PDI process by signing next to the explanation of why the additional work is required. • When a repair is not completed on the same day, the technician is taken off the job, or when the technician takes a lunch or personal break, the time off and the time resumed must be documented. All time recording must reflect the actual starting and ending times for the repair. NOTE: Digital documented warranty, campaign, and goodwill repairs do not require technician to punch on and off any job that is identified through a labor operation in the Labor Time Standard, as well as any Campaign/Recall or op code in a TSB. Straight Time (NTT, ATT), and claims (including Campaign/Recall) utilizing non-HMA LTS hours must still be supported by separate and distinguishable on/off punch times, see section 6.1.1. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 147

title: Time Recording for Service Performed by a Technician Team
raw_content: Time recording requirements and payroll documentation apply to all technicians– whether working individually or working as part of a team. The additional time recording requirements as they apply to technician team service operations are: • Each technician’s time (including the lead technician’s time) must be recorded separately by clocking on and off of a daily time ticket (flag sheet). • The information on each time flag must be completely filled out. This includes the repair order number, technician identification, the type of repair performed (customer pay, internal, or warranty) labor time hours, and the appropriate line on the repair order that corresponds with the repair performed. • The hard copy of the repair order must contain the name or number of the technician(s) who performed the repair alongside the description of the repair. Team only identification is not acceptable. • The Labor Time Standard applies regardless of technician pay plans or working arrangements. For example, a trainee technician may remove and reinstall a transmission while the actual repair is performed by an expert technician. Reimbursement is based on the applicable labor time standard operations regardless of the fact that two persons work on the repair at the same time or that the actual time required for the repair may be in excess of the labor time standard because of the use of a trainee technician. NOTE: Digital documented warranty, campaign, and goodwill repairs do not require technician to punch on and off any job that is identified through a labor operation in the Labor Time Standard, as well as any Campaign/Recall or op code in a TSB. Straight Time (NTT, ATT), and claims (including Campaign/Recall) utilizing non-HMA LTS hours must still be supported by separate and distinguishable on/off punch times, see section 6.1.1. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 148

title: Parts Substantiation
raw_content: Documentation must be provided to substantiate that a specific part stated on the warranty claim was used to repair the vehicle. The repair order must indicate and include the parts charged out to the repair order for a specific vehicle. For Dealers using an automated parts inventory system, a numbered counter requisition cross- referenced with the repair order or point of sale document and a date of transfer of parts from inventory, must be completed each time parts are removed from inventory. Each counter requisition number must be recorded on the repair order or the point of sale document. Every part removed from inventory must be listed on a counter requisition. Counter requisitions must be accounted for and filed in numeric sequence. All invoices or copies covering parts or services purchased from vendors or other shops (and a record of payment of such invoices), new car invoices and any other records used by the Dealer to substantiate the information recorded on the claim, must be retained by the Dealer for a period of five (5) years from date of claim payment. Repairs performed using other than Genuine Hyundai Parts purchased from HMA must have the non-genuine Hyundai part supplier’s name, invoice number, and part number recorded on all copies of the repair order including the customer’s receipt copy. The supplier’s invoice must be retained with other accounting files. The use of non-genuine Hyundai parts for warranty repairs requires prior approval from the District Parts and Service Manager. Non-genuine Hyundai Parts include Hyundai-branded parts that are not supplied by HMA and are intended for use outside the U.S. In the event that a requisitioned part is not utilized in a repair and returned to inventory, this fact must be documented. Dealers who utilize an electronic repair order system or a parts inventory control system must generate a monthly report which tracks all activity for parts returned to inventory. A copy of this adjustments to inventory report should be generated prior to the monthly close and retained in the Parts Department. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 149

title: Sublet Repair Record Requirements
raw_content: When sublet repairs are performed, the Dealer’s repair order must contain the following sublet final invoice cross-reference information: • Description of sublet repair • Name of sublet vendor repair shop • Date of sublet repair • Purchase order number issued for sublet repair • Sublet repair invoice number • Sublet amount The sublet repair final invoice must be itemized and contain the following repair order cross- reference information: • Dealer’s repair order number • Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) or vehicle license plate number • Date of sublet repair • Mileage of vehicle • Mileage vehicle towed (if applicable) • Purchase order number issued for sublet repair • Sublet amount • Reasons and details of repair performed • Detailed breakdown of part(s) and labor: • Detailed part(s) itemized • Detailed labor itemized The original copy of the sublet vendor final invoice must be attached to the accounting copy of the repair order. A copy of the sublet vendor final invoice must be attached to the technician input/shop hard copy of the repair order which is filed in the service files. All sublets require an final invoice as well as any other pertinent information to be attached. If the Dealer supplies the sublet vendor with the part(s) required to perform the sublet repair, the Dealer is to claim the part(s) on the warranty claim as a detail part(s) (enter part under Part Information section on WebDCS). The Dealer supplied part(s) is not to be shown or claimed on the vendor’s sublet repair final invoice. If the vendor supplies the part(s), the vendor supplied part(s) cost is to be included on the vendor’s sublet repair final invoice amount. The amount of sublet requested on the claim for reimbursement must be the actual net sublet cost incurred by the Dealership. All sublet amounts claimed must be the actual sublet amount cost paid to the sublet vendor, less any discounts or rebates. Note: Sublet amount over $100 will require Additional Comments to be entered in the warranty claim. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 150

Ensure sublet vendor final invoice is itemized and contains specific repair order cross-reference information (Dealer RO number, VIN/license, date, mileage, towed mileage if applicable, PO number, sublet amount, repair reasons/details, detailed part breakdown, detailed labor breakdown).

title: Supervision Responsibilities
raw_content: Service management is responsible for verifying that the repair order contains accurate in/out mileage, open/ close dates, owner-described symptoms and clear legible instructions, before issuing the technician input/ shop hard copy of the repair order to the technician. Service management is also responsible for: • Identifying on the technician input/shop hard copy of the repair order the specific employee name or number (supervision/salary/hourly) assigned to perform the warranty/customer satisfaction repair. • Verifying that the Service Advisor enters the nature of the customer’s complaint. • Verifying that the repairing technician enters the cause of the customer’s complaint and enters a description of the repair on the repair order technician input/shop hard copy (3 Cs). • Verifying that the correct nature and cause codes are entered on the repair order processing copy and warranty claim. • Entering on the repair order any additional repairs that are required but not discovered until after the repair order initial write-up. The additional repair entries must be signed by the Service Manager, next to each actual repair, to indicate approval of the additional repairs before the repairs are performed. (This signature is required even if the additional repairs are written-in by service management). • Signing starting “on” and completion “off” clocking times for actual straight time repairs. • Signing any hand written time recording entries. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 151

title: Customer Service File
raw_content: To administer the warranties, the Dealer must establish and maintain a customer/VIN service file. Maintaining appropriate records and supporting documents will enable the Dealer to service the owner better and minimize the possibility of comebacks and denied claims or charge-backs due to servicing ineligible vehicles. Files must be filed/organized by VIN.

Files must be filed/organized by VIN
claim_processing_risks: Denied claims due to servicing ineligible vehicles
Charge-backs due to servicing ineligible vehicles

title: Vehicle Identification Number File
raw_content: • Indicate the vehicle identification number (VIN) on the file folder tab for each new vehicle received. • Arrange folders in numerical sequence by the complete 17 digit VIN, the last six digits of the VIN, or the short VIN which is comprised of the 6th, 10th, and 12th through 17th digits of the complete 17 digit VIN. • Insert copies of all repair orders, signed Pre-Delivery Inspection forms, claims, Warranty Vehicle Information screen printouts, and other required documents in the corresponding folders by date as prepared. • Write the owner information and date of sale or date of first use on the folder tab as soon as available. The Vehicle Identification Number filing system allows for a file to be started upon completion of a new vehicle Pre-Delivery Inspection. This type of filing also allows for new and/or multiple owners, thereby better recognizing comeback repairs and vehicle service history. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 152

title: Warranty Audit Policy
raw_content: HMA may conduct audits of Dealer claims and claim supporting records in accordance with provisions of the Dealer Sales and Service Agreement (refer to Section 1). Records supporting wholesale parts purchases or parts incentives may also be examined during the audit. An audit does not imply that improper practices are being or have been engaged in, however, a chargeback will be made if the audit reveals that improper or unsubstantiated claims have been submitted by the Dealer and paid by HMA. HMA may also conduct audits of Dealer’s compliance of calibration and operation of shop tools and equipment in accordance with Manufacturer’s required calibration schedule. Improper and/or unsubstantiated claims include, but are not limited to, those claims involving: • Unauthorized replacement of assemblies or parts. • Misrepresentation of parts acquisition cost or sourcing of part. • Unauthorized use of non-Hyundai Genuine Parts. • Parts claimed at a rate in excess of parts purchased. • Parts received from Dealer Parts Department inventory prior to performance of repair. • Defective parts not returned to Parts Department by technician. • Special ordered parts not documented or indicated. • Incorrect labor operation. • Unauthorized add-on, repeat, duplicate, comeback or improper repairs or over repairs. • Unnecessary repairs performed which are not based on a customer complaint or repairs where no corrective action is taken, except as authorized by an HMA representative. • Customer signed Early Bird envelope without an attached RO hard copy. Early Bird envelope does not replace RO requirements. • Printed (or digitally captured using tablet) pre-workorders used for customer write-ups not attached to an RO hardcopy. Properly completed pre-workorders must be retained and attached to the RO hardcopy. • Printed (or digitally captured using tablet) Multi-Point Inspection (MPI) sheets not attached to an RO hardcopy. Properly completed MPI sheet(s) must be retained and attached to the RO hardcopy. • No customer signatures authorizing repair(s) or acknowledgement of repair completion. • Reprinted lost or damaged repair orders without signed authorization from the Service Manager. • Signed reprint without the original repair order. When utilizing reprints, the original repair order with customer signature at the time of original write up must be retained. • No technician remarks or incomplete technician remarks, including required before and after measurements/readings. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 153 • Unsubstantiated labor time. • No supporting documentation or inadequate supporting documentation. • Reconditioning of used vehicle inventory at HMA Warranty expense. • Charging HMA for shop materials. • Repairs not covered by warranty or campaign, repairs related to improper installation of an accessory, repairs related to an accident, to customer modification, neglect, abuse, or lack of maintenance, or any claims which are not Hyundai responsibility. • Misrepresented VIN, in-service date, repair date, mileage, causal part, labor operation, sublet, freight, or towing. • Misrepresented HMA authorization, deliberate claim splitting to avoid authorization or claims where payment received exceeds the amount authorized. • Duplicate submission and payment for a repair. • Related concerns for same repair on separate claims. • Work not performed as claimed. • Improper actual time recording to substantiate repair. • Parts not available for inspection, improperly completed Warranty Tag, not defective as alleged, or not applicable to model, body style or trim level. • Technician concurrently working on two or more vehicles or repair orders at one time. • Payroll records which do not support technician payment or amount of payment to technician. • Unauthorized mark-up of sublet, NPN parts, towing or freight. • Any claim in which repairs and/or record retention policy and procedures were not properly adhered to. • Any claim in which repairs and/or record retention policy and procedures were not properly adhered to. The list of examples shown above is not all-inclusive. The dealer has the responsibility to fully adhere to the Warranty Policy and Procedures provisions, including verifying that repairs are eligible and needed due to a warrantable defect, regardless of: • Prior Approval (PA) • On-site inspector approval • Digital imaging approval • Techline, Field Service Engineer involvement, etc. Company involvement, including prior repair approvals, does not exempt the repair from warranty audits, review, or chargeback. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 154

title: Warranty Audit Procedures
raw_content: HMA highly encourages Dealers to establish, maintain and operate an effective system of internal controls to meet HMA reporting and record keeping requirements. These internal controls are critical components of a Dealer’s parts & service management and can help Dealers achieve their goals and long-term profitability. Warranty audits not only cover the warranty aspect of the Dealer’s parts & service operation, but also provide an opportunity to assess the effectiveness of internal controls and evaluate any deficiencies and/ or discrepancies in a Dealer’s Warranty Policy and Procedures compliance. At the completion of the audit, all noncompliant audit findings are documented in the audit report and may result in a chargeback to the Dealer. In addition, noncompliant audit findings resulting from irregularities and/or discrepancies are included in the audit report as formal requirements and recommendations designed to assist the Dealer in operating a more efficient, profitable, and properly controlled parts & service operation. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 155
claim_processing_risks: Noncompliant audit findings may result in a chargeback to the Dealer.

title: Warranty Claim Register
raw_content: A Warranty Claim Register provides the Dealer with a summary of the status of the warranty accounts receivable from HMA. A properly maintained warranty claim register enables the Dealer to reconcile the Warranty Settlement Statement. Reconciliation of warranty claims to the Warranty Settlement Statement will indicate when credits for warranty adjustments are being received and whether or not there are any amount differences between amounts claimed and the amounts credited. The Warranty Claim Register should be administered as follows: • Post each warranty claim submitted to HMA to the Warranty Claim Register daily. The information posted should include at a minimum: – Repair Order Number – Claim Number assigned by Dealer – Warranty claim amount – Warranty claim initial submission date to HMA – Warranty claim return date, if applicable – Warranty claim return reason(s), if applicable – Warranty claim resubmission date(s), if applicable – Warranty claim payment date – Warranty claim payment amount – Warranty claim different appeal date(s), if applicable – Warranty parts request date, if applicable – Warranty parts return date, if applicable – Warranty date to scrap – Warranty date scrap initial – Warranty claim chargeback date, if applicable Posting to the Warranty Claim Register should be initiated when a warranty claim is submitted to HMA. Upon notification of claim status, such as payment or return, as per the Warranty Settlement Statement or by the DCS daily answerback (Claim Status Report), the claim’s status should be posted to the Warranty Claim Register. • Post the following data from the Warranty Settlement Statement to the Warranty Claim Register upon receipt of the Warranty Settlement Statement: – Payment/Return date – Claim credit amount • Note any discrepancies between the requested input data per the claim submitted over WebDCS and the Warranty Settlement Statement and add remarks, if necessary, for the difference in the Warranty Claim Register. Examples of discrepancies include: Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 156 – Errors or differences in the amount credited. – Claims returned for correction should have the date of return posted and the date of the corrected resubmission posted. – If a returned claim is not to be resubmitted, note it in the remarks section. The Warranty Claim Register and the Warranty Settlement Statement must be retained in the dealership accounting office files. If the Warranty Claim Register is automated, a copy must be printed monthly and retained. The Warranty Settlement Statement should be filed chronologically by statement date and month sequence. Hyundai Warranty Claim Registers are available free through the Hyundai Non-Parts Warehouse. The part number is NP449-MV010. An order quantity of one (1) provides ten (10) register sheets. Section 6 Warranty Record Retention & Administration Requirements 157
